Matching txids appear in both notification types (e.g. txid 0ddc338f397fa21e... -> both "Payment received -- 0.00012 BTC" and "Transaction confirmed -- ... 9602 confirmations").

Root cause (src/lib/server/addressWatcher.ts): baselineExisting() (line 637) is supposed to seed notified_txids with confirmed=1 for every pre-existing tx BEFORE state.baselined flips true, specifically to prevent this exact flood (see its own doc comment, line 631-636). It is a slow, fully sequential per-scripthash loop (await chain.electrum.getHistory(scripthash) one address at a time, up to WATCH_WINDOW*2 = 60 addresses per wallet), wrapped in a try/catch that silently skips any address whose history fetch fails (line 653-655) -- with no retry and no re-baseline pass.

handleNewBlock() (line 527) has NO state.baselined guard (unlike handleScripthashChange(), which correctly gates on it at line 390) -- it processes every notified_txids row with confirmed=0 on every single 'header' event, including headers re-emitted by reconnects.

Given cairn-u7bw, this instance's Electrum connection was reconnecting every ~90-120s throughout the observation window -- so baselineExisting()'s slow sequential scan is very likely to have individual getHistory() calls fail mid-pass when the socket drops, leaving those specific addresses' real history un-baselined (never inserted with confirmed=1). Once state.baselined flips true regardless, the next scripthash-change event for one of those addresses (e.g. triggered by a resubscribe after the next reconnect) runs handleScripthashChange() for real: the txid isn't in notified_txids yet, so it's treated as brand new, recordTxid() inserts it with the schema default confirmed=0, and it immediately fires "Payment received". The very next 'header' event's handleNewBlock() then picks up that same freshly-inserted confirmed=0 row, sees it already has thousands of real confirmations, and immediately fires "Transaction confirmed" for it too.

Impact: a real user reconnecting after any period of Electrum instability (or simply restarting the app on a wallet with substantial existing history) can see a confusing flood of "new payment" / "transaction confirmed" notifications for transactions that happened long ago and were never new -- this landed as 50+ notifications in a single admin instance in this session.

Fix shape: make baselineExisting() resilient to partial failure -- e.g. retry failed addresses on the next periodic refresh before allowing handleScripthashChange to treat them as live, or track baselined-state per-scripthash rather than one global state.baselined flag, so a mid-scan Electrum drop can't let stale history leak through as "new".
close_reason: Fixed in src/lib/server/addressWatcher.ts: baselined-state is now tracked PER SCRIPTHASH (state.baselinedScripthashes) instead of one global flag. baselineScripthash() only marks an address baselined after a fully successful history fetch; failures stay quarantined and are retried by refreshWatches' 5-min sweep AND on-demand when a change event arrives (which silently baselines instead of notifying, so stale history can never leak out as 'payment received'). handleNewBlock() now has the same !state.baselined startup gate as handleScripthashChange(). Bonus: newly subscribed wallets (incl. imports with history) are baselined by the retry sweep before going live. addressWatcher tests pass; full suite green (1445).

title: Surface derivation path more visibly in multisig 'Add keys' wizard step
description: Soft UX finding from 2026-07-06 derivation-path audit (docs/MULTISIG-DERIVATION-AUDIT-2026-07-06.md): the multisig creation wizard's main 'Add keys' step (src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.svelte, key 'slot' UI ~lines 1073-1098) shows device name/category/fingerprint for each cosigner but not the BIP-48 derivation path itself. Path is correctly stored/exported (descriptor export, ColdCard registration file) and used correctly for PSBT construction — this is purely a visibility nicety, not a correctness gap. Consider showing the path (e.g. m/48'/0'/0'/2') inline in each key slot, consistent with Cairn's plain-language UX philosophy (could show as a collapsed/secondary detail rather than headline).

title: Wire log.error() into remaining single-sig API route catch-alls
description:
Follow-up to cairn-obb (closed -- logger/handleError/request-logging infra all landed solidly). ~30 routes already call log.error() in their catch blocks (multisig broadcast, stateless ops, auth, admin restore, etc.) but a handful of single-sig routes were missed, most importantly:
- src/routes/api/wallets/[id]/transactions/[txId]/broadcast/+server.ts (~line 26-35) -- catches, returns 502, never logs
- src/routes/api/wallets/[id]/+server.ts (~line 29-31, wallet detail GET)
- src/routes/api/wallets/[id]/receive/+server.ts (~line 18-20)
- src/routes/api/wallets/[id]/psbt/+server.ts (~line 75-77)

Not silent (hooks.server.ts logs a generic 'METHOD path 502 Nms' line for every request unconditionally) but the actual error/stack trace is lost for these specific routes since the exception is fully handled locally and never reaches the handleError hook. Add the same log.error({err: e, ...context}, 'message') pattern already used elsewhere (e.g. the multisig broadcast endpoint) to these remaining routes -- single-sig send/broadcast is the single most common money-moving path in the app, it should have the best error visibility, not the worst.
close_reason: Verified in d5f9c73: all previously-bare catch-alls in the single-sig wallet routes (wallets/[id], addresses, psbt, receive, transactions, broadcast, bump, utxo-mass) now call log.error({err: e, ...context}, 'message') via the 'wallet' childLogger before responding, matching the pattern already used in multisig routes. Spot-checked the broadcast endpoint directly -- log.error({err: e, walletId, txId}, 'wallet broadcast failed') now fires in the unexpected-error branch, right before the generic 502 response.

title: Multisig wallet name concatenates imported config name with user-entered name
description: When creating a multisig wallet via 'Already have this wallet in another app? Import it' with a Caravan JSON that has a 'name' field (e.g. "P2WSH-M"), and then typing a different name in the Confirm step (e.g. "Caravan Test Vault"), the resulting wallet's stored name becomes the two strings concatenated with no separator: "P2WSH-MCaravan Test Vault". Confirmed via: wallet page title, /wallets list, and the raw Caravan export from GET /api/wallets/multisig/[id]/caravan (the exported name field itself contains the mashed-together string). Likely cause: the imported config's name pre-fills the name input as a starting value, and the wizard appends rather than replaces when the user edits it, or the create action concatenates the two sources server-side. Repro: import docs fixture src/lib/server/__fixtures__/caravan-2-of-2-P2WSH_MAINNET.json (name: P2WSH-M), then type any different name in step 4 and create.
close_reason:
RETRACTED: not a real bug. A deep code trace by a second audit agent (checked every site where an imported config's name meets a user-typed name: +page.svelte:506 pre-fill-if-empty, +page.server.ts create action, api/wallets/multisig/import/+server.ts's '|| parsed.name' fallback, and multisig.ts createMultisig) found no concatenation logic anywhere -- multisigName is a single  string with a plain bind:value (+page.svelte:1454), and line 506 only pre-fills it 'if (imported.name && !multisigName.trim())', i.e. only when the field is still empty.

Root cause of the original observation: during live browser testing, the Caravan import correctly pre-filled the name field with 'P2WSH-M' (matching the imported config's own name). At the Confirm step I then clicked into that already-populated field and typed 'Caravan Test Vault' WITHOUT first clearing the existing text -- standard browser input behavior inserts typed text at the cursor position rather than replacing existing content, which produced the literal string 'P2WSH-MCaravan Test Vault' that I then submitted. Every downstream artifact (UI title, wallets list, Caravan/ColdCard export, PDF backup) correctly reflects that literal string I typed -- they are not further evidence of an app bug, they're confirmation the app faithfully stored and echoed back exactly what was submitted.

This was a test-methodology error (should have select-all'd or cleared the pre-filled field before typing), not a Cairn defect. Filing a much lower-priority, clearly-separate UX suggestion (auto-select pre-filled name text on focus) as a follow-up since it would have prevented even my mistaken input, but retracting this issue as originally filed.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Confirmed the same bug propagates to two more export formats, tested via curl: (1) ColdCard export (GET /api/wallets/multisig/2/coldcard) shows 'Name: P2WSH-MCaravan Test' -- truncated even further (missing trailing ' Vault'), suggesting the ColdCard file format applies its own length cap on top of the already-concatenated name. (2) The printable PDF backup (GET /api/wallets/multisig/2/backup-pdf) bakes in the literal string 'P2WSH-MCaravan Test Vault' -- a user who prints this for physical/break-glass storage gets the mangled name permanently on paper. Raises severity: this is written into every durable backup artifact the wallet produces, not just a cosmetic UI issue.

title: Test gap still open: describe.runIf silent-skip trap door in multisigPsbt.test.ts
description: The 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit noted: 'describe.runIf(scriptTypeReady(...)) in multisigPsbt.test.ts:665-748 is a silent-skip trap door — if p2sh/p2sh-p2wsh multisig support regresses, ~50 lines of tests vanish with zero failures instead of failing loudly.' Re-verified 2026-07-06: describe.runIf is still used in src/lib/server/bitcoin/multisigPsbt.test.ts and src/lib/server/bitcoin/vaultRegtestE2E.test.ts. This means a regression in p2sh/p2sh-p2wsh multisig script-type support would silently reduce test coverage rather than fail CI. Replace the runtime-conditional skip with either an explicit CI-visible skip reason (e.g. it.skip with a loud comment reviewed regularly) or make scriptTypeReady's precondition always true in the test environment so the tests always run.

Always shown regardless of referral_links flag (troubleshooting resources are not admin-configurable/promotional).

title: Enforcement sweep: wire requireFeature() into every gated route
description: Add requireFeature(event, key) calls at the real server-side call sites for every flag in docs/FEATURE-FLAGS-PLAN.md §1.1: send API, multisig-creation routes, coin-control UTXO picker, CSV export route, address-book routes, QR camera entry points, wallet-config import/export routes, explorer routes, each src/lib/hw/* driver's registration path, each notification channel's send path. Largest/most scattered unit -- split into per-feature-area child beads as the sweep starts rather than guessing exact boundaries up front. This is the actual security boundary; §5 UI hiding alone is not enforcement.

title: Webhook HMAC signing secret has no minimum length/entropy check — a 1-char secret is accepted and used
description: src/lib/server/channels/webhook.ts's readUserConfig() only checks typeof secret === 'string' && secret.length > 0 before using it as the HMAC-SHA256 key for the X-Cairn-Signature header (createHmac('sha256', config.secret)). The write path (PUT /api/notifications/channels/webhook) has the identical bare emptiness check, and the UI's secret field is a plain password input with no minlength. webhook.test.ts explicitly exercises and expects a 1-character secret ('x') to work. A weak/short user-chosen secret makes the signature trivially forgeable, undermining the integrity guarantee X-Cairn-Signature is meant to provide to the receiving endpoint. Low severity since it's the user's own secret protecting their own receiver, not a Cairn-side credential. Fix: enforce a reasonable minimum length (e.g. 16+ chars) at both the API validation layer and with a UI hint/generator button.
close_reason: Fixed in 779a374

title: Umbrel auto-admin setup
description: Supersede the v1 decision in docs/PUBLISH-PLAN.md (§5/§7.3) to ship signup-only for Umbrel. Full design: docs/SOLO-MODE-UMBREL-AUTOADMIN-PLAN.md Part 1. bootstrapAdminFromEnv() (src/lib/server/auth.ts:237) already creates the first admin from CAIRN_ADMIN_PASSWORD/CAIRN_ADMIN_EMAIL at boot and the signup page already skips itself once userCount() > 0 — no code change needed for that part. What's missing: (1) Umbrel package wiring using a distinctly-named derived secret (not Umbrel's own APP_PASSWORD, which collides with Cairn's legacy break-glass alias), (2) a forced first-login password+email reset for bootstrap-created admins since the generated password lives in Umbrel's install UI/logs indefinitely, (3) doc update, (4) real-device test pass.

Pass it into docker-compose.yml as CAIRN_ADMIN_PASSWORD (and set CAIRN_ADMIN_EMAIL) so the existing bootstrapAdminFromEnv() (src/lib/server/auth.ts:237) consumes it unchanged. Set defaultUsername/defaultPassword in umbrel-app.yml so Umbrel's install-complete screen shows real credentials.

title: Forced first-login password+email reset for bootstrap-created admins
description: Add a users.must_reset_password-style flag set by bootstrapAdminFromEnv() when it creates a user from an env-supplied password. At login, if set, redirect to a one-time 'set your own password and email' step before reaching the dashboard. Needed because: (a) the generated password sits in Umbrel's install UI/logs indefinitely, (b) admin@cairn.local placeholder email means notification emails have nowhere real to go.

title: Update docs/PUBLISH-PLAN.md for new auto-admin decision

title: umbrelOS test pass: fresh install -> auto-admin -> forced reset -> persistence
description: Via the umbrel-test-app workflow: fresh install shows generated password -> login with it -> forced reset prompt appears -> set real password+email -> reaches dashboard -> restart container -> confirm reset password (not the generated one) persists and bootstrap doesn't re-fire or clobber it.

title: Product: standard Umbrel HTTP access blocks ALL direct hardware-wallet browser connect (secure-context restriction)
description:
Not a code bug -- Cairn already detects and handles this as gracefully as a web app can (clear in-app messaging + working file-based fallback) -- but worth recording as a product finding since it affects every real user on the standard access path, and combines badly with cairn-tu0i.

Umbrel's default/primary access URL for an installed app is plain HTTP on a .local hostname (confirmed live: http://umbrel.local:3211, no TLS). Browsers only expose WebUSB, WebHID, Web Serial, and allow the Trezor Connect popup handshake in a "secure context" -- HTTPS or literally "localhost". src/lib/hw/trezor.ts's isTrezorConnectAvailable() (window.isSecureContext), and the WebHID/WebSerial checks in ledger.ts/bitbox02.ts/jade.ts, all correctly reflect this browser restriction.

Consequence, reproduced live against the v0.1.3 Umbrel instance: every one of Trezor Connect (popup), Ledger (WebHID), BitBox02 (WebHID), and Jade-over-USB (Web Serial) reports itself unavailable on the standard http://umbrel.local:PORT URL -- i.e. for essentially every real Umbrel user who hasn't gone out of their way to set up their own HTTPS/tunnel/Tailscale access. Only the "Generic wallet / file" method and Jade's QR/animated-QR path remain usable.

This means the wizard's headline pitch ("Trezor -- Plug it in and connect with one click", "Ledger -- Plug it in and connect with one click") never actually works for a typical Umbrel user visiting the app the normal way -- they always land on the file-based fallback (or QR), whether or not they realize it's the fallback. Combined with cairn-tu0i (single-sig PSBTs never carry bip32Derivation), the file-based fallback is ALSO currently non-functional for real hardware signers that require key-origin info -- so hardware wallet signing for single-sig wallets is not currently usable via any path on a stock Umbrel install.

Worth Alex's product call on: (a) whether Umbrel offers/could offer an HTTPS local-access option worth documenting/defaulting to, (b) whether the wizard's device-picker copy should lead with the file/QR method as primary for Umbrel users rather than presenting hardware-popup connect as the first, most prominent option, and (c) prioritizing cairn-tu0i so at least the file-based fallback actually works end-to-end once this is understood.

title: Explorer address lookup rejects all regtest (bcrt1) addresses as invalid
description:
Page: /explorer/address/[address]

What I did:
- From a transaction detail page (/explorer/tx/78e33965...), clicked both an input address link (a P2WPKH-looking bcrt1q... address, 42ish chars) and an output address link (a P2WSH bcrt1q... address, 64 chars) — both are addresses that genuinely appear on-chain in this regtest instance's own transactions.

What happened:
- Both clicks land on the app's styled 404 page ("This path doesn't lead anywhere").
- This is NOT a routing 404 — confirmed the underlying SvelteKit data request (`/explorer/address/<addr>/__data.json`) returns HTTP 200 at the transport level, but its JSON payload explicitly encodes a logical error: `{"type":"error","error":{"message":"Not a valid Bitcoin address"},"status":404}`. So the server-side load function is deliberately rejecting these addresses as invalid.
- Both address types (P2WPKH-shaped and P2WSH-shaped) are rejected identically, so this isn't specific to script type — it looks like the address-validation helper only accepts mainnet-prefixed addresses (bc1.../1.../3...) and doesn't recognize the bcrt1 (regtest) or presumably tb1 (testnet) HRPs at all, even though this whole instance is connected to and displaying data from a regtest chain (block explorer, tx details, etc. all work fine and show bcrt1 addresses natively elsewhere in the app).
- Contrast: the Explorer search box's client-side heuristic happily classifies a bcrt1 string as "looks like an address" (tested earlier this session) — so there's a mismatch between the lenient client-side pattern-match and the stricter (and here, wrong) server-side validator.

What should have happened:
- Address validation should accept whatever network the instance is actually configured for (mainnet/testnet/regtest), not hardcode mainnet-only prefixes. Every address that appears anywhere else in this app's own UI (tx inputs/outputs, wallet receive addresses) should be clickable and loadable on its own detail page.

Priority: P1 — this breaks a core Explorer feature (address lookup) completely for any regtest-connected Cairn instance, which is exactly the setup used for hardware-wallet/vault testing throughout this project. Real-world testnet users would likely hit the same issue.
close_reason: Fixed in 1ce7d33: new network-agnostic isExplorerAddress (bech32/bech32m bc/tb/bcrt + testnet/regtest base58 versions) gates both explorer address routes; mainnet-only isValidAddress untouched for wallet code. 13 new tests incl. real regtest bcrt1q/bcrt1 accepted, plus an assertion that isValidAddress still rejects them. 632 tests green.

title: Signup form: native browser validation silently blocks submit with no visible error (required invite code field)
description:
On /signup, all four fields (displayName, email, password, inviteCode when shown) use bare HTML `required` attributes with no custom validation messaging. If a user tries to submit with any field empty/invalid, the browser's native tooltip validation kicks in (e.g. "Please fill out this field.") — there is no app-level error state, no red outline beyond the native browser default, and nothing in Cairn's own visual language. This is inconsistent with the polished custom "form-error" banner the page already has for server-side errors (see form?.error in +page.svelte).

Repro: go to /signup, leave "Invite code" blank (when invite-only registration mode requires it), fill everything else, click "Create account" — the button appears to do nothing except show the browser's default native validation bubble, easily mistaken for the button being broken.

Suggested fix: add client-side validation with Cairn-styled inline error messages instead of relying solely on native `required` attributes.
close_reason: Fixed in 1f94da6: signup form validates in the enhance handler (novalidate) and renders failures in the app form-error style, covering the required invite code case

title: Admin break-glass via env var (Umbrel)

Log PROMINENTLY via the pino logger: 'Admin recovery login via environment variable'. Umbrel context per cairn-auth-model memory (password mode must exist there anyway — this is for passkey-only instances).

title: README doesn't mention sending, hardware wallet signing, or the Vaults/multisig feature at all
description:
README.md "Features (v1)" section only lists block explorer, watch-only wallet navigator, multi-user, admin panel, and no-own-node operation -- the exact feature set from when the project was pure watch-only. Since then, Cairn shipped: PSBT construction and a full guided send flow, hardware wallet signing (Trezor Connect, Ledger WebHID, ColdCard SD-card, air-gapped QR), RBF fee-bumping, coin control, an address book, batch sending, and an entire multisig Vaults feature (guided creation wizard, per-key signing stepper, Caravan import/export interop, Ledger policy registration, key health checks, signing-mass estimation for hardware-wallet verification time).

None of that appears anywhere in the README. A visitor reading it today would have no idea Cairn can send a transaction at all, let alone sign with a hardware wallet or manage a multisig vault -- this is the single biggest gap between what Cairn actually does and what it says it does. For a public repo, the README is the product pitch; right now it undersells the project by roughly half its actual capability.

Also smaller, same-file: no vault screenshot in the existing screenshot grid (docs/screenshots/dashboard.png, block-detail.png, mempool-blocks.png, wallets.png exist; nothing for send flow, hardware signing, or vaults), and the "Bitcoin Core RPC -- optional, stored for upcoming features" line should be reviewed for accuracy now that broadcast supports Core RPC as a backend.

Fix: rewrite the Features section to cover the full current surface, add 1-2 screenshots covering the send flow and a vault, and verify the Core RPC line still describes current behavior rather than a stale "upcoming" state.
close_reason: Committed: full features rewrite in the existing voice with current terminology (multisig wallet, never vault), stack additions verified against package.json, Core RPC line corrected against actual code (Electrum-only broadcast; bead's speculation was wrong and is documented), two new real screenshots (send-review, multisig-wallet) captured at matching resolution/theme, stale alt text fixed.

Need to: (1) git rm --cached .claude/launch.json, (2) add .claude/ to .gitignore, (3) confirm no other .claude/ artifacts get committed going forward (also watch for .beads/ which is now initialized and must be handled per project policy on whether the tracker itself is meant to be public).

title: README.md is unedited sv/SvelteKit scaffold boilerplate
description: README.md still contains the default 'sv create' template content (generic 'Creating a project' / 'Developing' / 'Building' sections referencing npx sv create). It has no project description, no explanation of what Cairn is, no setup instructions specific to this app (env vars, data dir, invite-based registration, electrum/esplora config), no screenshots. A public-facing OSS repo needs a real README from commit one.

title: Send flow doesn't move focus on step transitions
description: src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte: every step change (create->review, review->sign, sign->confirm, confirm->sent, and step = 'review' etc. via the back buttons) just flips the step state; focus stays wherever it was (typically the button that was clicked). On a 5-step, irreversible-money-movement flow this is a real problem, not cosmetic: a screen reader user gets no announcement that the page moved to a new step, and a keyboard user has to manually navigate to find the new content instead of picking up where the flow put them. Fix: on each step change, move focus to the new step's heading/lead element (e.g. a focusable heading with tabindex=-1 that gets .focus() called), so both keyboard and screen-reader users land on the new step's content automatically.
close_reason: Fixed in 7c944da: every step section has tabindex=-1 with a position+name label; a single effect on step state moves focus after tick() for all transitions including programmatic advances; initial load and resume don't steal focus; focus ring suppressed for programmatic focus, preserved for :focus-visible.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Verified fixed (commit 7c944da). Live-tested per S5's specific ask (tab/screen-reader through the flow, confirm focus lands sensibly after each transition):

- Initial page load: focus stays on body (correctly does NOT steal focus on load, matching the fix description).
- Create -> Review (clicked "Review transaction"): focus moved to <section class="step-body" tabindex="-1" aria-label="Step 2 of 5: Review">. Confirmed via document.activeElement.
- Review -> Sign (clicked "Looks good — sign"): focus moved to the Sign step's section, aria-label="Step 3 of 5: Sign".
- Confirmed via source that the same tabindex="-1" aria-label={stepAriaLabel} pattern is applied identically on all 5 step sections (Create/Review/Sign/Confirm/Sent), driven by one generic $effect keyed on the `step` state and firing after tick() so it targets real DOM -- not per-step special-cased code, so Confirm and Sent (which I couldn't reach live; same recurring test-data gap -- no wallet here has both real balance and a known signing key) get the identical treatment as the three transitions I did exercise directly.
- Focus-ring handling confirmed too: .step-body:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none } -- suppresses the ring for the programmatic focus() call while preserving it for real keyboard :focus-visible use.

This is solid: single mechanism, generically applied, verified directly on 3 of 5 transitions and by code inspection on the rest.

title: Notification content & delivery-quality audit 2026-07-05
description: Epic tracking findings from a full content/formatting/delivery audit of the notification system (docs/NOTIFICATION-PLAN.md). Plumbing already tested (see cairn-notification-system-testing memory); this pass is about whether the notifications themselves are good — content quality, per-channel formatting, batching, actionability, priority, quiet hours. Children below.
close_reason: Tracking epic: all 8 children (cairn-5gpv.1 through .8) closed — deep-links fix, HTML email templates, batching/digest, quiet hours + priority, quorum-met notification, password-change security notification, device tracking, and admin coverage gaps all landed. Nothing left to track.

title: Deep links broken on every external notification channel — relative path sent with no origin
description:
notify() payloads carry payload.link as a bare relative app path (e.g. \"/wallets/3\", \"/admin/users\" — see notifyTypes.ts doc comment and every call site in addressWatcher.ts, auth.ts, keyHealth.ts, multisigRoster.ts, rateLimit.ts, webauthn.ts, chainEvents.ts). In-app rendering (NotificationPanel.svelte) resolves it fine because it's an <a href> inside the app's own origin.

But every EXTERNAL channel ships that same relative path into a context with no implicit origin:
- email.ts:256 appends `\n\n${payload.link}` to the plain-text body — a bare \"/wallets/3\" in an email is not a clickable/resolvable URL in any mail client.
- telegram.ts:63 appends it as plain HTML-escaped text — Telegram only auto-links full URLs, so this renders as inert text.
- ntfy.ts:102 sets `body.click = payload.link` — ntfy's `click` action expects an absolute URL to open in a browser; a relative path is undefined behavior at best.
- nostr.ts:169 joins it into the DM plaintext — same inert-text problem as Telegram.

Fix: build one shared helper (e.g. in notifyTypes.ts or a new notifyLinks.ts) that joins payload.link against the instance's public origin, and have every external channel call it instead of using payload.link raw. The origin config already exists and is already used for exactly this purpose elsewhere: `env.CAIRN_ORIGIN` (see src/lib/server/webauthn.ts:47, `env.CAIRN_ORIGIN ?? event.url.origin`). notify() runs outside a request context in most call sites (background watchers, queue), so there's no `event.url` to fall back to — CAIRN_ORIGIN must be the source of truth here, and the helper should omit the link entirely (rather than send a broken one) when CAIRN_ORIGIN is unset, with an admin-facing hint to set it for self-hosted instances that want working notification links.

Webhook (webhook.ts:127-137) ships payload.link verbatim in structured JSON — lower priority since a script consumer can prepend its own known base URL, but worth documenting the field as relative in the header comment (already partially documented) and optionally offering an absolute variant too.
close_reason: Fixed in commit: shared notifyLinks.absoluteNotificationLink() resolves payload.link against CAIRN_ORIGIN; email/telegram/ntfy/nostr now send absolute URLs (omitted when CAIRN_ORIGIN unset, with admin hint); already-absolute links pass through; webhook body gains linkAbsolute. Unit test notifyLinks.test.ts. check + 969 tests green.

title: Email notifications are plain-text only — no HTML template, branding, or CTA button
description:
email.ts's sendMail() (line 229) calls transporter.sendMail({from, to, subject, text}) — no html field at all. Every email notification is the raw payload.title as subject and payload.body (+link) as a plaintext body. No Cairn branding, no formatted amounts/tables, no button back to the app.

Per the audit brief, email is the one channel that should be the most full-featured (full HTML template, branding, detailed context, links back to the app) precisely because it's asynchronous and the user isn't already in the app the way they are for in-app notifications.

Fix: add an HTML alternative part (nodemailer's `html` field alongside `text`) with:
- minimal branded header (Cairn wordmark/color, matching the app's dark theme where email clients allow it)
- the title as a heading, body as prose
- a clear \"View in Cairn\" button linking the absolute URL (depends on cairn-5gpv.1 for a working link)
- plain-text part kept for accessibility/dark-client parity and reused as-is when a PGP key is on file (keep the encrypted path plain-text only — encrypting HTML adds complexity for no benefit here, per email.ts's existing PGP branch at line 259)

Scope this to a shared template function (subject, title, body, link) so all 13 event types get the same treatment without per-event work.
close_reason: Added channels/emailTemplate.ts (shared HTML+text renderer: branded dark-theme header, title heading, body prose, 'View in Cairn' button on the absolute link, level-based accent). email.ts now sends html+text alternatives on the cleartext path; PGP path stays plain-text only (encrypts renderText). Covers all event types via one template. Tests added.

title: No batching/digest for burst events — N confirmations in one block fire N separate external sends
description:
addressWatcher.ts's handleNewBlock() (line 488) loops over every pending notified_txids row and fires one notify({type:'tx_confirmed', ...}) per row with no grouping. A user with 10 UTXOs confirming in the same block gets 10 separate notify() calls -> 10 separate in-app rows AND 10 separate queued sends per enabled external channel (email/telegram/ntfy/etc.), each its own message. The notificationQueue's per-channel token bucket (5/sec, notificationQueue.ts) throttles delivery pacing but does not deduplicate or summarize — the user still receives 10 emails/pushes back-to-back instead of one \"10 transactions confirmed\" summary.

Same shape risk in addressWatcher's tx_received loop over multiple watched addresses touched in one Electrum callback.

Fix: for at least the external channels (in-app can reasonably stay one-row-per-tx since it's a browsable list), add a short coalescing window per (user, event_type) — e.g. buffer newly-queued external-channel rows for ~30-60s and collapse same-type rows for the same user into one digest send (\"3 transactions confirmed in Vault: ...\") before they leave notify()/the queue. Webhook payloads may want to stay one-JSON-object-per-event for automation consumers — consider excluding webhook from batching or emitting an array-body digest there instead.
close_reason: Queue worker now coalesces same (user, channel, event_type) bursts of batchable types (tx_confirmed, tx_received) into ONE digest send per group ('3 transactions confirmed'), instead of N back-to-back external messages. Webhook excluded (automation wants one JSON/event); in-app unaffected (browsable list). Groups of 1 send individually. Digest failures fall back to per-row retry state machine. Tests added.

title: No quiet hours and no priority-aware delivery — all levels queue/send identically
description:
Two related gaps found reading notificationQueue.ts, notifyTypes.ts, and the notification_preferences / notification_channel_config schema (db.ts) — neither table has any column for this, and there's no UI for it in settings/notifications/+page.svelte.

1. No quiet hours: a user cannot set a do-not-disturb window. A 3am tx_confirmed push is exactly as loud as a 3pm one. Users who want push notifications for security events but not routine confirmations overnight have no way to express that today short of disabling the event type entirely (losing the routine notifications too, not just deferring them).

2. No priority-aware delivery: the queue (notificationQueue.ts tick()/processRow()) drains 'pending' rows strictly FIFO by id, rate-limited per-channel at a flat 5/sec — a P0 admin_server_health 'error' row queued behind a burst of routine tx_received 'info' rows waits its turn like everything else. ntfy.ts is the ONLY channel that reads payload.level at all (priorityForLevel() maps error->5/warn->4/info-success->3, ntfy.ts:51); email, telegram, webhook, nostr ignore level entirely — no X-Priority/Importance email header, no Telegram silent/loud distinction, no visual urgency cue.

Fix (two independent, both worth doing):
- Add a per-user quiet-hours window (start/end local time, maybe a "still notify on security/error-level events" override) that the queue worker checks before calling plugin.send(), deferring non-urgent rows until the window ends rather than dropping them.
- Have the queue select/process 'warn'/'error' rows ahead of 'info'/'success' rows within a tick (a simple ORDER BY case-when-level, not a full priority queue rewrite), and extend email.ts to set an Importance/X-Priority header for warn/error levels the way ntfy already maps priority.
close_reason: Quiet hours: new user_notification_settings table + quietHours.ts (tz-aware wrap-around window, quietDecision) + /api/notifications/quiet-hours route + settings UI section. Queue worker defers routine (info/success) rows to the window end (attempts unchanged); warn/error bypass per urgent-override. Priority-aware delivery: queue tick now processes warn/error rows ahead of info/success (level parsed from payload); email.ts sets Importance/X-Priority headers for warn/error. Tests added.

title: Password changes fire no security notification (unlike new-passkey)
description:
webauthn.ts:230 fires a security_new_passkey notify() (\"was this you?\") whenever a passkey is added. auth.ts's setUserPassword() (line 146) — called from the user's own Settings page at src/routes/(app)/settings/+page.server.ts:83 to let a user change their password — has zero notify() call. Password is Cairn's default auth method (see cairn-auth-model memory: dual auth, password is the default, Umbrel needs it) so a silent password change is a bigger blind spot than the passkey case it already handles: an attacker who takes over a session and changes the password to lock the real owner out currently generates no signal on any channel.

Fix: add a notify({type: 'security_password_changed', level: 'warn', title: 'Password changed', body: '...if this wasn't you, ...'}) call at the setUserPassword() call site in settings/+page.server.ts (or inside setUserPassword itself, mirroring how webauthn.ts does it inline). Needs a new NOTIFICATION_EVENT_TYPES entry + DEFAULT_PREFERENCES row (default in-app, same as security_new_passkey) + an entry in the Security group of settings/notifications/+page.svelte's GROUPS catalogue + NotificationPanel.svelte's ICON map.

Separately (larger, not in scope of this bead): there is no \"new device / new location login\" notification at all, and none is possible today without adding device/IP tracking — the sessions table (db.ts:37) stores only token_hash/user_id/created_at/expires_at, no user_agent or IP. Worth a follow-up feature bead if Alex wants that coverage; noted here so it isn't lost.
close_reason: Added security_password_changed event type (notifyTypes + DEFAULT_PREFERENCES ['inapp'] + activity USER_FEED_TYPES + NotificationPanel ICON + Security group in settings/notifications). Fires a warn-level 'was this you?' notify() at the setUserPassword() call site in settings/+page.server.ts, but only for an actual change (not first-password-on-passkey-only).

title: No 'device tracking' — new-device/new-location login can't be notified today
description:
Follow-up split out of cairn-5gpv.5. The audit brief asks whether \"new device\" login is covered by a security notification — it isn't, and can't be cheaply added, because the sessions table (db.ts:37: id, token_hash, user_id, created_at, expires_at) has no user_agent/IP/device-fingerprint column to compare a new login against.

Scope if picked up: add user_agent + first-seen IP (or a coarser device fingerprint) to sessions on creation, keep a small \"known devices\" set per user (could piggyback on DevicePicker.svelte's existing device-labeling UX for consistency), and fire a new security_new_device notify() the first time a session is created from an unrecognized fingerprint. Bigger lift than the other beads in this epic — P3, not blocking the rest of the audit's fixes.
close_reason: Added device tracking: sessions.user_agent/ip_address columns + known_devices table + deviceTracking.ts (coarse UA fingerprint, security_new_device notify on unrecognized fingerprint, never on the user's FIRST device). createSession now takes an optional SessionContext; threaded sessionContextFrom(event) through all login/register/recover session-creation sites + the settings password-change site. New event type wired into notifyTypes/DEFAULT_PREFERENCES/activity/UI/panel. Tests added.

title: No 'quorum met / ready to broadcast' notification — only 'signature waiting' exists
description:
multisigRoster.ts:86 fires sign_session_waiting (\"A transaction from '{name}' is waiting for your signature\") to each cosigner who hasn't signed yet. There is no companion event for when the LAST required signature arrives and the PSBT is fully signed / ready to broadcast — checked NOTIFICATION_EVENT_TYPES (notifyTypes.ts) and every notify() call site; nothing fires at quorum-met.

This is the single most actionable moment in the whole signing flow (someone needs to go broadcast it) and today it's silent — a wallet's initiator has to keep checking the UI to notice quorum was reached, with no push/email/in-app nudge.

Fix: add a sign_session_complete (or similar) event type, fired from wherever multisigRoster.ts / the PSBT-collection code detects `collected >= required` for the first time, notifying at minimum the wallet's creator/initiator (and arguably every cosigner, so they know their signature landed) with a link straight to the broadcast action.

Related smaller content gap, same area: the existing sign_session_waiting body (\"A transaction from '{name}' is waiting for your signature.\") doesn't surface quorum progress even though multisigRoster.ts:92 already computes `collected`/`required` and puts them in `detail` — the audit brief specifically calls out \"quorum progress\" as expected content. Cheap fix alongside the above: change the body to something like \"...is waiting for your signature (2 of 3 collected).\"
close_reason: Added sign_session_complete event: fired from attachMultisigSignature the instant a session reaches quorum (guarded by pre-attach progress so it fires exactly once, on the transition into completeness), notifying every roster member with a broadcast deep link. Registered the type in notifyTypes, DEFAULT_PREFERENCES, the NotificationPanel icon map, and the settings toggle. Also enriched the sign_session_waiting body with quorum progress ('(N of M collected)'). Test added in multisigRoster.test.ts. (Registry-file additions were surgically staged to avoid committing a concurrent session's in-flight notification work in the same files.)

title: Admin notification coverage gaps: user disabled, instance settings changed
description:
Current admin event types (notifyTypes.ts): admin_new_signup, admin_invite_used, admin_restore, admin_server_health. Checked every admin route under src/routes/(app)/admin/ for notify() call sites — none exist for:
- an admin disabling/re-enabling a user account (a meaningful moderation/security action other admins should see)
- instance-wide settings changes (SMTP relay reconfigured, Electrum/node endpoint changed, webhook SSRF allow-list toggled, etc.) — several of these are security-relevant (e.g. webhook_allow_private_targets flip) and currently leave only a generic audit-log trail (per cairn-observability), not a notification any admin gets pushed.

Lower priority than the other beads in this epic (P3) since these are admin-only and already covered by the activity/audit log — but worth adding for multi-admin instances where one admin should know when another changes something security-relevant without having to go check /admin/logs.
close_reason: Added admin_user_disabled + admin_settings_changed event types (notifyTypes + DEFAULT_PREFERENCES + settings/notifications Admin group + NotificationPanel icons). Wired notify() broadcasts (userId:null → all admins): admin/users disable/enable action fires admin_user_disabled; api/admin/notifications POST fires admin_settings_changed only when the webhook SSRF allow-private-targets toggle actually flips.

title: Ledger, ColdCard, and QR signers are built and tested but never wired into the Sign step's method list
description:
Page: /wallets/[id]/send (Sign step)

What I did: after commits 7655343 (signer contract), 87c6ba6 (Ledger WebHID), e1d3d52 (ColdCard SD-card flow), and 677c767 (QR/BBQr) all landed, navigated through Create -> Review -> Sign on a real draft to see whether Ledger, ColdCard, and QR / Animated now appear as selectable signing methods.

What happened: all four device cards (Trezor, Ledger, ColdCard, QR / Animated) still render as disabled "Coming soon" placeholders, identical to before these commits landed. Confirmed via source: src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte still has a hardcoded `comingSoonDevices` array (line ~286) listing all four names and rendering each through the generic disabled `DeviceCard` component (line ~609-611) — it never imports or references `LedgerSigner.svelte`, `ColdCardSigner.svelte`, `QrSigner.svelte`, or `signerContract.ts`'s `DeviceMethod`/`available()` pattern anywhere in the file.

This isn't a bug in the new components themselves — I independently verified all three work correctly (Ledger: 25 passing unit tests covering accountOriginFromPsbt/mergeSignatures/error translation; ColdCard: file-reading logic verified against a real binary .psbt from Cairn's own /file endpoint, correct on binary/text/empty-file cases; QR/BBQr: full device round-trip proof — encode to frames, simulate a shuffled camera scan, sign with a real BIP84 test-vector private key, encode the signed result back to frames, scan again, and the result passes both `assertSameTransaction` and `finalizePsbt` — using Fable's actual bbqr.ts, not a mock). The components and their underlying logic are solid; they're just not reachable by a real user because the parent Sign step's method list was never updated to offer them.

What should happen: `send/+page.svelte` should replace the ColdCard, Ledger, and QR entries in `comingSoonDevices` with the real `ColdCardSigner`/`LedgerSigner`/`QrSigner` components (gated by each one's `available()` check per signerContract.ts's own design, e.g. Ledger only offered when WebHID is present), leaving only Trezor in the disabled list since no TrezorSigner.svelte exists yet.

Priority: P1 — three complete, well-tested features are currently invisible to users; this is the one remaining step to ship them.
close_reason: Fixed in 0f59954 (Ledger/ColdCard/QR wired as live capability-gated method cards through the shared substitution guard) and a092c0f (Trezor). No coming-soon tiles remain; agent live-verified method expand/collapse and guard routing in the browser.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Verified fixed. Commit 0f59954 "feat: wire device signers into the Sign step" wires all built signers (Ledger, ColdCard, QR) into send/+page.svelte's method list via a proper DeviceMethod[] + component map, gated by each method's available() check — matching signerContract.ts's intended design exactly as described in this bead.

Live-tested through the browser (Create -> Review -> Sign on wallet 2 "Legacy wallet"):
- Generic wallet/file, Ledger, ColdCard (microSD), and Animated QR all now render as real selectable methods instead of disabled placeholders.
- Trezor also landed in the meantime (commit 5f2d59a "feat: Trezor Connect PSBT signing") and is wired in too — all 4 previously-"coming soon" devices are now real, selectable, working method cards. Nothing remains hardcoded as a placeholder.
- Confirmed the substitution guard (assertSameTransaction) fires correctly through both the newly-wired ColdCard file-upload path and the QR manual-paste path: uploading a PSBT with a tampered output amount is rejected with "The signed transaction pays different outputs than the one you reviewed. It was not accepted."
- Confirmed the completeness check works too: uploading a still-unsigned PSBT is rejected with "This PSBT was attached but still is not fully signed."
- Confirmed Ledger and Trezor both show a clear device-verification panel ("check the address on the device screen") before attempting to sign.

One related but separate follow-up filed as cairn-sta (P2): ColdCard and QR signer cards don't proactively warn when a wallet lacks stored key-origin data (no master_fingerprint => no bip32Derivation), unlike Ledger which already surfaces a clear error for this case. Not a security issue — just a UX gap where a user could walk through several minutes of physical air-gapped device steps for a signing attempt that's predictably doomed.

Closing this one out — great fix.

title: Account recovery for passkey accounts
description: Recovery methods for passkey-enabled accounts, critical for admins. HARD UX RULE at every step: ACCOUNT recovery (Cairn login) vs BITCOIN recovery (hardware seed) must be impossible to confuse — required copy: 'Your recovery phrase restores your Cairn account only — not your bitcoin. Your bitcoin keys are safe on your hardware wallet regardless.' and 'Store your recovery phrase separately from your hardware wallet backup — they protect different things.' SECURITY NOTE for implementers: this is auth-critical code — build with fresh context, review carefully, follow existing auth.ts idioms (scrypt, timingSafeEqual, hashed storage only).
close_reason: Epic complete: all 5 children shipped (fbb7efd core phrase/codes/break-glass, 659e24c mandatory setup, 23962a5 /recover page). Adversarial security review ran (verdict ship); its medium timing-oracle finding on the code path was fixed before commit (constant scrypt work). 661 tests green, check clean.

title: Verify, test, and commit the uncommitted BitBox02 multisig-registration fix
description:
Security audit 2026-07-05 (docs/SECURITY-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md), finding F6.

At audit start, src/lib/hw/bitbox02.ts signPsbtWithBitbox02() called paired.btcSignPSBT(...) directly for multisig PSBTs with no prior call to btcRegisterScriptConfig/btcIsScriptConfigRegistered -- the code's own comment explicitly deferred this ('Unit F', not yet built). This would have been a P0/P1 (trust-model integrity: the BitBox02 could sign without the device ever showing the real quorum/cosigner policy on-screen; funds themselves stayed safe only because server-side finalizeMultisigPsbt anchors the spent script to server-stored config).

While this audit was in progress, a fix landed in the working tree (uncommitted as of this writing): a new maybeRegisterMultisig() helper (checks btcIsScriptConfigRegistered, calls btcRegisterScriptConfig if needed) is now called before btcSignPSBT whenever the script config is multisig, plus new regression tests in bitbox02.test.ts asserting the check-then-register-then-sign call order for: not-yet-registered, already-registered (register skipped), and single-sig (neither call happens). 3/3 independent adversarial verification agents confirmed the fix is real and correctly implemented, refuting the original finding against current working-tree state.

Action needed: run the full test suite to confirm bitbox02.test.ts passes, sanity-check the fix against real BitBox02 hardware if available, then commit src/lib/hw/bitbox02.ts, src/lib/hw/bitbox02.test.ts, and src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/send/_components/MultisigBitboxSigner.svelte together (currently uncommitted modified files per git status). Until committed, this fix could be accidentally lost (e.g. a git reset/checkout by another session) and the gap would silently reopen.
close_reason: Fixed in security-audit remediation pass; see commit. All 897 tests pass, typecheck clean.

title: Test suite is red: rename refactor broke coldcardName truncation test
description:
npm test currently fails (608 passed, 1 failed) -- src/lib/server/multisigExport.test.ts "coldcardName > truncates to 20 characters".

Root cause confirmed: commit 78a2864 ("refactor!: rename \"vault\" to multisig wallet across the codebase") did a text substitution that also changed the test's own input fixture string from "An extremely long vault name here" to "An extremely long multisig name here" -- but did not update the corresponding truncated expected-output assertion, which still reads .toBe("An extremely long va") (truncating "vault"). The renamed input's first 20 characters are now "An extremely long mu" (from "multisig"), so the assertion fails. coldcardName() itself (src/lib/server/multisigExport.ts) is unchanged and correct -- this is purely a stale test fixture, not a functional regression.

Fix: update the expected value to "An extremely long mu" (and verify the second assertion in the same test, .length toBeLessThanOrEqual(20), still holds -- it does, unaffected).

Flagging as P1 despite being a one-line fix: an audit-gate pass should never find the test suite red. Whoever merged 78a2864 either did not run the full suite afterward or did not read the failure -- worth a quick process note for whichever session/agent did the rename, not just the one-line fix itself.
close_reason: Fixed: expected truncation updated to match the renamed fixture ('An extremely long mu'). Suite green again: 610/610.

title: Admin > Server logs crashes the entire app: Svelte each_key_duplicate on identical log lines
description:
Page: /admin/logs

What I did:
- As admin, navigated to Admin > Logs (the server log viewer, backed by C:\dev\cairn\data\logs\cairn.log).

What happened:
- The page reliably crashes on load. Console shows a fatal, uncaught Svelte error:
  "Svelte error: each_key_duplicate — Keyed each block has duplicate key `{"level":20,"time":1783259192211,"tag":"electrum","height":127,"msg":"new block"}` at indexes 350 and 351" (see https://svelte.dev/e/each_key_duplicate), thrown from src/routes/(app)/admin/logs/+page.svelte's keyed {#each} over log lines.
- Root cause: two log entries in the log file are byte-for-byte identical (same millisecond timestamp, same tag "electrum", same message "new block", same height 127) — plausibly from a duplicate emit during an electrum reconnect/watcher restart. The {#each} is apparently keyed by the full log-entry object/its JSON shape rather than a stable unique id (e.g. line number or a monotonic sequence), so two identical entries collide and Svelte throws instead of rendering.
- Blast radius is much bigger than just this page: the fatal error appears to crash the whole client-side app shell, not just the logs component. Confirmed via console logs: right after the each_key_duplicate exception, the sidebar nav renders completely empty (no Dashboard/Explorer/Wallets/etc. links) even though the rest of the page chrome loads, and a *subsequent, unrelated* navigation to /explorer failed with an unstyled framework-default "500 Internal Error" page (not Cairn's own branded error page) plus a cascade of "Failed to fetch dynamically imported module" errors — consistent with the client module graph getting into a broken state after the uncaught render exception.
- Reloading /admin/logs reproduces the crash every time (log file still has the duplicate entry): the page header and controls (level filter, search box, Refresh button, "Showing 899 of 899 recent lines") render, but the actual log list area is completely blank, and the sidebar nav is empty.

What should have happened:
- Log lines should be keyed by something guaranteed-unique (line index/offset, or an incrementing sequence number), not by content-derived identity, so duplicate/near-duplicate log entries (which are a completely normal, expected occurrence in a live log stream) can never crash rendering.
- Even if a rendering error occurs, it should be contained to the logs page/component (a Svelte error boundary) rather than taking down the whole app shell and leaving unrelated pages broken.

Priority: P1 — this is a core admin/operability feature (explicitly part of this polish pass) that is currently completely unusable, and its failure mode leaks into unrelated pages of the app.
close_reason: Fixed: the {#each} over log lines keyed by raw text; byte-identical lines (duplicate 'new block' on electrum reconnect) threw each_key_duplicate and crashed the app shell. Now keyed by stable list position; expand-set rekeyed. Browser-verified as admin: /admin/logs renders 956 lines (incl. the duplicates) with nav intact, no console errors.

title: Unify PSBT spend-rule validation between psbt.ts and multisigPsbt.ts
description: validateRecipientsAndFeeRate() and selectSpendCandidates() in src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.ts replace the recipient/fee, coin-eligibility, and coinbase-maturity blocks multisigPsbt.ts copied verbatim (its own comment admitted it). Messages and error codes byte-identical.
close_reason: Committed in bf996d7; svelte-check clean, all 263 bitcoin tests + full suite pass

title: resetInstance() factory reset leaves instance-wide events (user_id IS NULL) behind, contradicting nothing else survives claim in Settings UI
description: src/lib/server/admin.ts resetInstance() runs DELETE FROM wallets/invites/sessions/users/settings in a transaction and relies on ON DELETE CASCADE (users(id)) to clean up everything else. But src/lib/server/db.ts L424 defines events.user_id as nullable with a comment NULL = instance-wide - rows with user_id IS NULL have no FK target to cascade from, and resetInstance() never issues a DELETE FROM events. Confirmed in src/lib/server/activity.ts: recordActivity() writes instance-wide rows (userId omitted/null) for things like chain/electrum switches, backups, restores. The admin settings page danger-zone copy tells the admin a reset deletes all users, sessions, wallets, and invites... nothing else survives - that is false for instance-wide activity history, which a new operator will see in /admin/activity after a reset, including entries like instance restore applied from the prior operator/session. Fix: add DELETE FROM events to resetInstance()'s transaction.
close_reason: Fixed in 46dd16f

title: Missing regression test: pino redact config for secrets/xpubs/PSBTs/mnemonics (cairn-byoz)
description: cairn-byoz (P2, closed in 7ebd68c) added a pino redact config (confirmed present at src/lib/server/logger.ts:180-228, REDACT_KEYS + redact.paths/censor) as defense-in-depth so a future logger.error({...}) call with a password/xprv/mnemonic/token field can't leak the raw secret into data/logs/cairn.log (which /admin/logs then serves over HTTP). No logger.test.ts exists to verify the redaction actually works. Add one: log an object containing a top-level and one-level-nested secret-shaped key (e.g. {password: 'x', nested: {xprv: 'y'}}) through a captured pino destination/stream, and assert the serialized output shows a redaction marker, not the raw value, at both nesting levels.
close_reason: Regression tests committed in 56a1371 (security-logging.test.ts, auth.test.ts setSessionCookie block, logger.test.ts + exported REDACT_OPTIONS, agreement/server.test.ts, api/wallets/server.test.ts, (app)/layout.server.test.ts).

title: Privacy disclosure + self-service data rights
description:
docs/DATA-AUDIT-2026-07-06.md sections 7/9/11: the user agreement (disclosures.ts) covers
custody/liability well but says nothing about server-side data handling -- IP logging, the
activity feed, admin log access, or that an admin can generate a full-instance backup
containing every user's wallet xpubs. Separately, there is no self-service account deletion,
no data-export endpoint, and no user-facing view of known_devices/sessions (both tables are
currently write-only from the user's perspective). Children: D1-D4 below.
close_reason: All 4 children done: agreement data-handling section (v2 bump), self-service account deletion, data export, Your devices page.

title: Add a data-handling section to the user agreement
description:
src/lib/server/disclosures.ts's DEFAULT_USER_AGREEMENT (lines 25-37) covers the custody/
liability model well (no key custody, backups are the user's responsibility, transactions are
irreversible, no liability for losses) but says nothing about server-side data handling: IP
logging on login/agreement-acceptance (admin_disclosure_acceptances/user_agreement_acceptances,
db.ts:385; disclosures.ts:122-128 for the best-effort client IP capture), the activity/events
feed, the notification delivery queue, or that admins can read /admin/logs (which, per this
audit's logging findings, can include other users' emails/IPs before that fix lands, and
userIds after). This is a custody/liability disclosure, not a privacy disclosure -- the two
are conflated where only the former exists today.

Fix: add a short "What we log and why" section to DEFAULT_USER_AGREEMENT (or a new
DEFAULT_PRIVACY_NOTICE constant following the same settings-table-backed pattern as
K_TEXT/K_OPERATOR/K_VERSION at disclosures.ts:12-14) covering: IP address logging (login
attempts, agreement acceptance), the activity feed, the scope of admin log access, and how
long data is kept (reference the retention behavior once the retention epic lands). Also
mention that an instance admin can generate a full-instance backup (src/lib/server/backup.ts's
buildBackup, lines 49-68) containing every user's wallet xpubs and address-book/labels -- a
reasonable disaster-recovery feature, but one a non-operator user on a shared instance should
know exists. Bump user_agreement_version so existing users are re-prompted to accept,
following whatever version-bump mechanism disclosures.ts already uses for text changes.

Acceptance criteria:
- Updated agreement text renders wherever the agreement is currently surfaced to users.
- The version bump triggers the existing re-acceptance flow for already-accepted users;
  extend disclosures.test.ts to cover it.
close_reason: Data-handling section added to DEFAULT_USER_AGREEMENT; DEFAULT_AGREEMENT_VERSION=2 + ensureDefaultAgreementVersion startup bump re-prompts stock-text instances; customized agreements untouched; disclosures.test.ts covers content + re-acceptance + idempotence. Committed.

title: Self-service account deletion
description:
No route anywhere lets a user delete their own account -- only admin deleteUser/resetInstance
exist (src/lib/server/admin.ts:75-109). Add a user-initiated "delete my account" flow.

Fix: new route (e.g. under Settings, a danger-zone section) + a deleteOwnAccount(userId)
function (in admin.ts or a new accountDeletion.ts) that reuses deleteUser's cascade logic but
scoped to locals.user.id instead of an admin-supplied target id. Use a two-step, typed
confirmation, mirroring the existing pattern for the admin "reset instance" danger-zone action
(cairn-21f) rather than a single confirm dialog.

Acceptance criteria:
- A user can delete their own account after typed confirmation.
- All rows the user owns are gone: wallets, multisigs, sessions, user_credentials, saved
  addresses, notification config, activity, etc.
- For multisigs where this user was a viewer/cosigner (not owner) via multisig_shares: the
  user's share row is removed, but the multisig itself survives intact for the owner and any
  other shares.
- Add accountDeletion.test.ts covering both the owner-deletes-own-wallet case and the
  shared-multisig-membership-removal case.
close_reason: deleteOwnAccount + Settings danger-zone flow with typed DELETE confirmation; cascade + no-FK cleanup + last-admin guard; accountDeletion.test.ts covers owned-wallet deletion, shared-multisig share removal, last-admin refusal. Committed.

title: Self-service data export (JSON dump of own data)
description:
The only "export" routes today are wallet-scoped (CSV transaction history, wallet-config/
descriptor backups) -- no "download everything Cairn has stored about me" export exists. Per
the audit's section 11, the export should include: profile (email, display_name, created_at),
wallets/multisigs metadata (names, xpubs -- never private keys, Cairn never has them),
transaction history, saved addresses and labels, notification preferences, activity feed, and
known devices/sessions for this user only -- i.e. everything in
docs/DATA-AUDIT-2026-07-06.md except password/recovery hashes and other users' data.

Fix: new route (e.g. GET /api/account/export, surfaced from Settings) that assembles a JSON
bundle by calling the same per-table read functions already used elsewhere (wallets.ts,
wallets/multisig.ts, addressBook.ts, activity.ts's user-scoped listing, notification
preferences, deviceTracking.ts/sessions for this user only), reusing the "no secrets"
projection pattern already established in src/lib/server/backup.ts (lines 49-68) but scoped
to locals.user.id instead of instance-wide.

Acceptance criteria:
- Exported JSON covers every table/field listed in docs/DATA-AUDIT-2026-07-06.md except
  password/recovery hashes and other users' rows -- use the audit as the checklist.
- Add accountExport.test.ts asserting the export is scoped strictly to the requesting user
  (seed a second user's data and confirm none of it appears).
close_reason: GET /api/account/export via buildAccountExport (audit §11 checklist, query-level user scoping, secrets redacted/excluded); accountExport.test.ts pins scoping + no-secret-material. Committed.

title: Add a 'Your devices' page (known_devices/sessions with revoke)
description:
src/lib/server/deviceTracking.ts's known_devices table and the sessions table are both
currently write-only from the user's perspective -- no UI ever reads them back, and there is
no way for a user to see or revoke their own active sessions/devices. The new-device alert
exists, but nothing lets a user act on it afterward. This turns write-only surveillance data
into an actual security feature the user can use.

Fix: add a Settings -> "Your devices" page listing sessions (created_at, user_agent -- avoid
showing raw IP directly to the user, to not over-expose it beyond what's needed) and
known_devices (first_seen, last_seen, user_agent), each with a "revoke"/"forget" action that
deletes the corresponding row for that user only.

Acceptance criteria:
- A user can view and revoke their own sessions/devices from Settings.
- Revoking a session invalidates it immediately (verify against auth.ts's session-validation
  path -- the next request with that token must be rejected).
- Add a test confirming a user cannot revoke another user's session/device by guessing an id
  (must be scoped to the requesting user's own rows only).
Note: pairs with the retention-epic's known_devices 12-month TTL bead -- that purges stale
entries automatically; this bead gives the user visibility/control over the rest. Do both.
close_reason: Settings -> Your devices: session/device listing with revoke/forget, user-scoped deletes (foreign-id no-op test-pinned), immediate invalidation via getSessionUser verified, current session flagged. Committed.

title: README needs real screenshots
description: README.md has zero images — pure text. For a project whose whole pitch is a polished self-hosted explorer UI, screenshots (dashboard, explorer block detail, mempool visualizer, wallet import wizard) would do more to sell the project than any amount of prose, and are pretty much expected for any GitHub README trying to attract self-hosters/stargazers. Capture a handful of key screens (dark theme, real or realistic data) and embed them near the top of the README.
close_reason: Implemented in 393e119: four real dark-theme screenshots (dashboard, block 956679 detail, mempool visualizer, wallet detail with live history) captured from the running app via CDP-driven headless Edge at 1440x900@2x, embedded as a 2x2 table under the README tagline

title: Batch sending (multiple recipients per transaction)
description: PSBT construction accepts exactly one recipient (src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.ts ConstructParams.recipient is a single string/amount, not an array), and the Create step form matches that. Paying multiple recipients in one transaction (payroll-style, or consolidating several sends into one fee) needs constructPsbt to accept a recipients array and selectUTXO to be called with multiple outputs -- the underlying @scure/btc-signer selectUTXO call already accepts an array of outputs per its signature, so this is more UI/API-surface work than new coin-selection logic. Backlog -- real feature, not urgent, larger UI surface than the other gaps found this round (recipient list add/remove, per-recipient amount validation, review step redesign for N outputs).
close_reason: Shipped: recipients array through constructPsbt/API/UI (max-with-multiple rejected), guarded recipients JSON column with NULL-for-single-sends and garbage-tolerant read-back, batch Review/Confirm rendering with single-recipient markup unchanged, batch RBF bumps supported and tested. 15 new tests; 366/366 passing.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Verified with a real emulator round trip (commit 38679b2). Built a 2-recipient batch send (15,000 + 8,000 sats) combined with coin control (single input from an allowlisted UTXO), signed with the real Trezor emulator, attached and broadcast via Cairn's live API.

Confirmed via the PATCH summary and the final broadcast response: both recipient outputs present with the correct amounts, plus change -- 15,000 + 8,000 + 6,140 change + 860 fee = 30,000 input, reconciles exactly. Real regtest txid: f421c3f85638584daf25caba1812f8c2397f81400e52f8903ff48683fef9dda2.

No issues found. Full suite: 366/366 passing.

title: Merge BitBox02 signer pair (BitboxSigner/MultisigBitboxSigner) into one component
description: Follow the merged Trezor pattern (multisig?: MultisigContext prop). BitboxSigner.svelte (419 lines) vs MultisigBitboxSigner.svelte (538) — both carry the pairing-code flow; the multisig variant adds script-config registration. Preserve all copy/flows/error handling exactly; svelte-check + vitest + preview load of both send pages; ideally hardware-test.
close_reason: Merged into src/lib/components/signing/BitboxSigner.svelte (multisig?: MultisigContext with ourKeyIndex); script-config registration and pairing flow preserved; both call sites updated; svelte-check 0 errors, 1120 vitest passing

title: Raw driver/exception text leaks to clients on unrecognized error paths (SMTP test, wallets.ts, stateless routes)
description: Three related sites forward raw driver/exception text to clients on unrecognized error paths: (a) src/routes/api/notifications/channels/email/test-smtp/+server.ts:100-103 and src/routes/api/admin/notifications/test-smtp/+server.ts:23-26 return raw err.message from an uncaught SMTP-connection exception verbatim to the client (the user-facing route accepts unsaved, user-typed host/port with no SSRF guard, so this could be a mild network-reconnaissance oracle); (b) src/lib/server/wallets.ts:160-165 createWallet rethrows raw DB driver errors for any failure other than a UNIQUE constraint violation, which src/routes/api/wallets/+server.ts:19-24 forwards verbatim to the client at 400; (c) the four src/routes/api/stateless/* routes (accepting untrusted pasted PSBT/Caravan/descriptor text) fall back to raw e.message at 502 for any exception not matching one of four known domain-error classes. Fix: consolidate to one reviewed "safe-to-forward" error classifier instead of the current instanceof-Error-then-forward-message idiom repeated across roughly 50 call sites; at minimum fix these three concrete instances. Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Fixed the three concrete instances in 22ab66d: both test-smtp routes and the stateless 502 fallback now return a generic message (raw error only logged); createWallet throws a sanitized message on non-UNIQUE DB errors. A single shared classifier across all ~50 sites remains a larger refactor, out of scope here.

title: No end-to-end test for M=1 or M=N (unanimous) multisig quorum boundary
description: All multisig tests (multisig.test.ts, multisigPsbt.test.ts, multisigTransactions.test.ts, stateless.test.ts) exclusively use strict M-less-than-N configs (2-of-3, 3-of-5) -- no test exercises a 1-of-N or N-of-N (unanimous) quorum. Off-by-one threshold bugs in signature-counting/finalization logic live exactly at these boundaries. Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Added explicit quorum-boundary tests to multisigPsbt.test.ts: M=1 (1-of-3 — not complete at 0 sigs and refuses finalize; completes+finalizes on the first signature from ANY single cosigner, verified with the third key) and M=N unanimous (2-of-2 needs both; 3-of-3 only completes on the third, refusing finalize at 1 and 2). Exercises the off-by-one threshold edges every prior lifecycle test (2-of-3, 3-of-5) skipped. All pass.

title: webhook SSRF blocklist misses IPv4-mapped IPv6 in compressed-hex form (::ffff:7f00:1), only checks dotted-quad spelling
description: src/lib/server/channels/webhook.ts's isBlockedAddress() regex /^::ffff:(\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+)$/ only matches the dotted-quad textual spelling of an IPv4-mapped IPv6 address (e.g. ::ffff:127.0.0.1). But WHATWG URL parsing normalizes a bracketed literal to the canonical compressed-hex form: new URL('http://[::ffff:127.0.0.1]/').hostname === '[::ffff:7f00:1]' (verified). That form doesn't match the regex, doesn't match any other branch (::1, ::, fc00::/7, fe80::/10), so isBlockedAddress falls through to 'not blocked' — a live bypass letting a webhook target loopback/private addresses via a bracketed IPv6-mapped literal (or an AAAA record returning the same canonical form). This directly contradicts the file's own doc comment claiming ::ffff:a.b.c.d is 'unwrapped and re-checked as IPv4' — it only handles one textual spelling, not the one the code's own URL parsing (and DNS resolution) actually produces. The existing test suite (webhook.test.ts) only feeds the dotted-quad form directly to the internal function, never the canonical hex form through an end-to-end checkTargetUrl() call, so this has no failing test today. Fix: normalize any ::ffff:HHHH:HHHH hex-form IPv4-mapped address back to dotted-quad (or compare numerically) before/within isBlockedAddress, and add a test exercising checkTargetUrl() with the bracketed literal end-to-end.
close_reason: Fixed in fc8da90

title: Coinbase-maturity check can be silently bypassed on a chain-RPC hiccup
description: src/lib/server/bitcoin/coinbaseScan.ts:16-29, isCoinbaseTx/annotateCoinbase: catches any chain-fetch failure and returns false (not coinbase) with zero logging and no caching of the failure. A transient Electrum/Esplora hiccup can make an immature mining-reward UTXO look ordinary and spendable, silently bypassing the 100-block maturity check added in commit 1dcc5bd. Fix: on fetch failure, propagate an explicit "unknown/unverifiable" state (not a silent "safe to spend" false) and log a warning. Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Fixed in c17328b

title: Solo mode / progressive disclosure
description: Out of the box, Cairn should behave as a single-user app: no invite system, no user list, no contacts, no wallet-sharing/cosigner-role UI visible. Admin unlocks these as they get comfortable. Full design: docs/SOLO-MODE-UMBREL-AUTOADMIN-PLAN.md Part 2. NOT modeled as a FEATURE_FLAGS registry entry — that registry (src/lib/server/featureFlags/registry.ts) is compiler-enforced default-enabled-only (a flag can never ship pre-disabled, to protect upgraders), which is the wrong invariant for 'new installs start narrow.' Audited all 23 existing flags: none gate multi-user surface (they're all capability switches — send, multisig_create, per-HW-driver, per-notification-channel, etc. — and must stay as-is). The actual multi-user surfaces (/admin/users, /admin/invites, /settings/contacts + api/contacts, multisig wallet sharing + api/wallets/multisig/[id]/shares) are currently completely ungated by any flag. Recommendation: new instanceMode: 'solo' | 'team' setting in settings.ts, alongside the existing registrationMode pattern, with upgrade migration logic so existing installs already using these features are unaffected.
close_reason: All 4 units + the cairn-7t0z.5 follow-up decision built and verified 2026-07-06. See docs/SOLO-MODE-UMBREL-AUTOADMIN-PLAN.md Part 2 and memory cairn-solo-mode-umbrel-autoadmin-plan for details.

title: instanceMode setting (solo|team) + upgrade migration
description: Add instanceMode: 'solo' | 'team' setting in src/lib/server/settings.ts alongside registrationMode. Migration logic on upgrade: new install (no users yet) -> 'solo'. Existing install -> 'team' if users count > 1, or any row exists in multisig_shares/invites/contacts; else 'solo' (a lone existing user isn't using these, narrowing their nav removes nothing).
close_reason: Built: instanceMode 'solo'|'team' in types.ts/settings.ts + one-time startup migration (src/lib/server/instanceModeMigration.ts, wired into hooks.server.ts). Heuristic: team if users>1 or any multisig_shares/invites/contacts row, else solo. Idempotent — never re-decides once a row exists. Tests in instanceModeMigration.test.ts (7 passing).

title: Gate /admin/users and /admin/invites behind instanceMode
description: Hide the Users and Invites tabs in src/routes/(app)/admin/+layout.svelte and guard the routes themselves (src/routes/(app)/admin/users, src/routes/(app)/admin/invites, api/admin/invites) so they only render/respond when instanceMode === 'team'. Not a requireFeature() 403 with 'disabled by administrator' messaging -- this is a narrower nav, not a disabled capability.
close_reason: Built: assertTeamMode()/requireTeamMode() guards in src/lib/server/api.ts (404, not 403). Applied to admin/users +page.server.ts (load + all actions) and admin/invites +page.server.ts (load + create/revoke). Nav tabs filtered in admin/+layout.svelte via data.instanceMode. Verified live: team-mode DB shows both tabs, solo-mode hides them and 404s the routes.

title: Gate contacts + multisig wallet-sharing behind instanceMode
description: Hide /settings/contacts (+ api/contacts/*) and the multisig wallet-sharing/cosigner UI (+ api/wallets/multisig/[id]/shares) when instanceMode === 'solo'. Same non-punitive hide (not the feature-flag 'disabled by administrator' pattern) as the admin/users+invites gate.
close_reason: Built: requireTeamMode() applied to api/contacts/+server.ts (GET/POST), api/contacts/[id]/+server.ts (PATCH/DELETE), settings/contacts/+page.server.ts (load), and the full multisig-share management API (api/wallets/multisig/[id]/shares[/shareId] — GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE). Deliberately does NOT gate getViewableMultisig or any other existing-access read path (cairn-7t0z.5).

title: Unlock team features admin control + solo-mode nudge copy
description: Add an 'Unlock team features' toggle in /admin/settings next to the existing registrationMode radio group, flipping instanceMode solo->team (reversible, non-destructive -- toggling back to solo re-hides nav without deleting data). Add a small nudge on the multisig wallet page for solo-mode users, e.g. 'Want to share this wallet with a co-signer? Turn on team features in Settings' -- so the capability doesn't vanish without a trail back to it.
close_reason: Built: 'Unlock team features'/'Hide team features again' toggle in admin/settings/+page.server.ts (unlockTeamMode/lockTeamMode actions) + +page.svelte section. Nudge copy on /settings Contacts section when solo, linking admins to /admin/settings. Verified live via preview tools, toggling both directions.

title: Decide behavior when toggling team->solo with active cosigner shares
description: Gap noticed while finalizing the solo-mode design (docs/SOLO-MODE-UMBREL-AUTOADMIN-PLAN.md Part 2, cairn-7t0z). The plan says toggling instanceMode back to 'solo' is non-destructive and 'just re-hides the nav' — but if the multisig-share API routes (api/wallets/multisig/[id]/shares) are gated on instanceMode === 'team', a cosigner who already has access would lose it the moment the owner flips the instance back to solo, even though the owner never meant to revoke that specific cosigner. Needs a decision: (a) gate only the *management* UI/API (inviting new shares) on instanceMode, but let already-granted shares keep resolving regardless of instanceMode, or (b) block instanceMode from flipping back to solo at all while any active multisig_shares/invites rows exist, surfacing that as a blocking message instead of a silent revoke. Leaning (a): a cosigner's existing access shouldn't depend on the owner's later nav preference. Decide before wiring the API-level gate in cairn-7t0z.3.
close_reason: Decided (a): gate only management UI/API (admin/users, admin/invites, contacts, share-creation endpoints) on instanceMode; existing cosigner read/sign access and logins are never gated by it. Documented in docs/SOLO-MODE-UMBREL-AUTOADMIN-PLAN.md Part 2 follow-up note.

title: notified_txids dedup key omits user_id — will silently suppress tx notifications for co-owners once collaborative custody ships
