title: Add Dockerfile, compose file, and deployment docs
description:
No containerization exists at all — no Dockerfile, no docker-compose.yml. Self-hosters (this app's entire audience) commonly expect a Docker path, especially for Umbrel-style one-click deployment. Needs:
- Multi-stage Dockerfile (Node 22.5+ base per package.json engines, adapter-node production build)
- docker-compose.yml with a documented volume mount for CAIRN_DB persistence (currently ./data/cairn.db; a container restart without a mounted volume silently loses all users/wallets/invites)
- 'start' script in package.json (currently missing — only dev/build/preview exist; production convention is npm start -> node build)
- README 'Deployment' section covering the Docker path, the CAIRN_DB volume requirement explicitly, and documenting the full set of environment variables adapter-node/the app respects (PORT, CAIRN_DB, and whatever adapter-node exposes — HOST, SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT etc. — currently undocumented beyond a vague mention of two of them).
close_reason: Implemented in 393e119: multi-stage node:22-alpine Dockerfile (non-root, CAIRN_DB=/data, PORT, ADDRESS_HEADER=x-forwarded-for for the rate limiter, HEALTHCHECK, VOLUME /data), docker-compose.yml with documented ./data bind mount and data-loss warning, npm start script, README Deployment section incl. env table, proxy header trust caveat, and SSE buffering note. Verified beyond inspection: docker build succeeded, container ran healthy as non-root, /api/health 200 from host, compose config validates

title: Missing regression test: removing a contact doesn't revoke their multisig wallet shares (cairn-2oex)
description: cairn-2oex (P1, closed in 016acf2) fixed contact removal not revoking a contact's existing multisig wallet shares — a real access-control gap (a removed contact could retain viewer/cosigner access). Fix confirmed present, no test exists. Add a test to src/lib/server/contacts.test.ts: create a contact, share a multisig wallet with them (viewer or cosigner role with an assigned key), call removeContact(), and assert the multisig_shares row is gone / assigned_user_id is cleared and the (former) contact's access role for that multisig is now null.
close_reason: Committed in 8603ac6: multisigShares.test.ts (first coverage), contacts.test.ts share-revocation, notifications.test.ts stage isolation, broadcast route malformed-JSON 400 + log.error tests, walletsDraft.test.ts buildDraft/coin-control coverage.

title: Missing regression test: agreement page reserved-action crash (cairn-z6i1)
description: cairn-z6i1 (P0, closed in 46dd16f) fixed /agreement crashing with a 500 because a Svelte form action was named 'default', a reserved name. Fix confirmed still present in current code. No test exists guarding against this class of regression. Add a test that POSTs to the agreement page's accept action (or a route-level test on /agreement) and asserts a 200/redirect rather than a 500 — this would catch a future accidental rename back to a reserved action name. Filed from the 2026-07-06 process retrospective (docs/PROCESS-RETROSPECTIVE-2026-07-06.md), which found only 6% of bug fixes added a regression test.
close_reason: Regression tests committed in 56a1371 (security-logging.test.ts, auth.test.ts setSessionCookie block, logger.test.ts + exported REDACT_OPTIONS, agreement/server.test.ts, api/wallets/server.test.ts, (app)/layout.server.test.ts).

title: Backup restore + account-reclaim chain allows registration-lockdown bypass to full admin
description:
Security audit 2026-07-05 (docs/SECURITY-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md), finding F7. Verified CONFIRMED by 3/3 independent adversarial reviewers.

Two independently-reasonable pieces of code chain into a full privilege escalation:

1. src/lib/server/backup.ts restoreBackup() (~lines 182-199): imports each user row from the decrypted backup file and writes u.is_admin verbatim with NO cap check against adminCount() and no distinct callout in RestoreSummary or the admin/restore route's log line. Imported rows have zero credentials by design (backups never carry credentials).

2. src/routes/api/auth/register/options/+server.ts (~lines 26-40) and register/verify/+server.ts (~lines 61-79): the account-reclaim path (reclaimableUserId() finds a credential-less user row) completely bypasses assertCanRegister() -- i.e. skips registrationMode (closed/invite/open) and invite-code checks entirely, by design, so a legitimate account can be reclaimed via passkey registration without an invite even when the instance is locked down.

Chain: attacker crafts/tampers with a backup file containing a row {email: attacker-controlled, is_admin: 1, no credentials}. An admin is social-engineered into restoring it via the admin-gated /api/admin/restore endpoint (looks like normal maintenance -- no preview of which imported rows are privileged). The attacker then visits the normal signup screen with that email; reclaimableUserId finds the credential-less admin row and routes around assertCanRegister entirely, regardless of registrationMode='closed'. Attacker completes a WebAuthn passkey ceremony with their own device, register/verify attaches the credential and immediately opens a live admin session. No invite code, no confirmation step, no admin-count cap, and no distinct audit-log/notification callout anywhere in the path (the only signup-notification, admin_new_signup, is never called on the reclaim branch).

Recommended fix (per verifier consensus):
- restoreBackup should force imported users' is_admin to 0, OR cap/require explicit admin re-confirmation for any is_admin=true row on import.
- The reclaim path should still honor registrationMode/invite-code for any account carrying is_admin=1, or require an additional out-of-band confirmation for admin-account reclaim.
- Fire a distinct, non-suppressible notification/audit entry whenever a restore imports an is_admin=1 row, and whenever any reclaim (not just admin) completes.
close_reason: Fixed in security-audit remediation pass; see commit. All 897 tests pass, typecheck clean.

title: Wallet creation/import emits no activity event (user feed or admin log)
description: Creating or importing a wallet (single-sig wizard, multisig wizard) generates no event in the user activity feed (/activity) or the admin activity log. It's a significant account action and should be recorded in both, following the existing event-emission conventions used by other features (see notification/activity plumbing).
close_reason: Fixed: createWallet emits wallet_added and createMultisig emits wallet_created (with quorum + created/imported wording) via recordActivity — both types were already whitelisted in USER_FEED_TYPES and have icons in /activity + the bell, so they now appear in the user feed and the admin activity log. Detail carries wallet identity only, never the xpub. Tests added in wallets.test.ts and wallets-multisig.test.ts (feed + admin log + no-event-on-failure + privacy assertion).

title: Mandatory backup download step on wallet creation
description: LAST wizard step for BOTH single-sig and multisig creation: 'Download your wallet configuration' — cannot dismiss/continue without downloading. Warning: 'This file is the only way to recover this wallet if Cairn's data is lost... Without it, you may permanently lose access to your bitcoin.' Multisig adds: 'your multisig wallet requires ALL key information to reconstruct. Your hardware wallets alone aren't enough without this file.' Single-sig wallet creation currently has NO such step at all — check the import/create flow.
close_reason: done

comments.author: auditor
comments.text: Audit note: closed with reason 'done' (no detail). Backfilling: fixed in 7c9ab8b — single-sig wizard gained a mandatory final 'back up your wallet' step (new /api/wallets/:id/config export, gated 'Go to your wallet' CTA); multisig wizard's existing caravan/coldcard/descriptor downloads now call markBackedUp and gate its finish CTA the same way. Verified: auth/ownership checks correct (requireUser + getWallet(user.id, id) scoping), config export contains only public data (xpub/script type/key origin, no private material). Gap: no test coverage was added for the new backups.ts module or the /api/wallets/[id]/config endpoint despite this being financial-safety-critical code — filing a follow-up bead.

title: Test gap still open: legacy p2pkh/p2sh-p2wpkh untested as spending source in psbt.test.ts
description: The 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit noted: 'Legacy p2pkh / p2sh-p2wpkh are never tested as the source wallet type in psbt.test.ts (only as destinations) — the nonWitnessUtxo-required and redeemScript-attachment branches for spending from these types are unexercised.' Re-verified 2026-07-06: still true. These are Bitcoin-critical boundary conditions on fund-moving code. Add test cases to psbt.test.ts constructing a PSBT that spends FROM a p2pkh and a p2sh-p2wpkh input, asserting nonWitnessUtxo is attached for p2pkh and redeemScript is attached for p2sh-p2wpkh.
close_reason: Committed in 26af83d: multisig substitution guard tests, legacy p2pkh/p2sh-p2wpkh spending-source tests, all 6 SPV reason codes, describe.runIf trap doors removed (multisig suites unconditional; regtest E2E skips loudly), coinbaseScan.test.ts fail-closed coverage.

title: Announcements: register announcement_banners feature flag
description: Add announcement_banners to FEATURE_FLAGS registry (src/lib/server/featureFlags/registry.ts), 'marketing' category (shared addition with cairn-gb5z if that lands first). When off, main layout must render zero banners and /admin/announcements should show a FeatureDisabled state.
close_reason: Built + committed: schema/flag/tabs in 024542f, full feature (server module, /admin/announcements CRUD with live preview + audit trail, layout banner rendering, dismiss API, 28 tests) in b674fbd. Full suite + svelte-check green.

title: Broadcast has a check-then-act race on the already-sent guard
description:
src/lib/server/transactions.ts broadcastTransaction(): reads the transaction row and checks status/txid (lines 263-266), then later writes the resulting txid back (lines 310-314) -- with a real await (the Electrum broadcast call) in between and no database transaction wrapping the read-check-write sequence. Two concurrent requests to POST /api/wallets/[id]/transactions/[txId]/broadcast (double-click bypassing the client-side disable, a retried request, or two tabs) can both read txid=null, both pass the already-sent check, and both broadcast.

Scoped correctly: this is NOT a double-spend-different-funds risk, since assertSameTransaction still guarantees both concurrent requests would be broadcasting the identical reviewed transaction (same inputs/outputs) -- worst case is submitting the same tx to the network twice (which Electrum/Core typically just reject as already-known) and a confusing DB-write race on which response the user's browser sees. Still worth closing: wrap the read-check-write in a single database transaction (or an UPDATE ... WHERE txid IS NULL returning affected-row-count as the atomic guard) so only one of two concurrent requests can ever proceed past the check.
close_reason: Fixed in 8717710: guarded UPDATE claim (txid IS NULL + no live claim, 60s stale expiry) before the Electrum await; loser gets already_sent; failed broadcast releases the claim. Concurrent-race test proves exactly one network call.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Verified fixed (commit 8717710, atomic guarded UPDATE claim before the network await). Per S5's specific ask (confirm it actually blocks a rapid double-click, not just narrows the window):

Read the dedicated concurrency test ("lets exactly one of two concurrent broadcasts through (atomic claim)") -- it's a rigorous simulation, not a token check: it parks the FIRST broadcast call's mocked Electrum send on an unresolved promise (so the first call has already run the atomic claim UPDATE and is now "in flight" exactly like a real slow network call), then fires the second call while the first is still pending. Second call correctly gets rejected with already_sent even though no txid has been written yet -- that's the actual race condition, not a simplified version of it. First call then completes normally when its network promise resolves.

Could not reproduce this via a genuine rapid double-click through the live browser UI -- reaching the real atomic-claim code path requires a fully-signed, finalizable PSBT, and (same recurring constraint) no wallet in this dev environment has both a real spendable balance and a known private key to produce one. The unit test's simulation is faithful to the real race though: claim-then-await-network is exactly the code path being exercised, with a genuinely unresolved promise standing in for the slow network call.

Also confirmed the failure-path claim release (UPDATE ... SET broadcast_started_at = NULL on a failed broadcast) has its own passing test ensuring a failed broadcast stays retryable, and the 60s stale-claim expiry is in the WHERE clause for crash recovery. Full suite: 301/301 passing.

title: OP_RETURN output labeled 'Unspent' — should be Unspendable/N-A
description:
On a transaction detail page, an OP_RETURN output is shown with status "Unspent", the same label used for genuinely spendable-but-not-yet-spent outputs. OP_RETURN outputs are provably unspendable by consensus rules, so labeling one "Unspent" is technically incorrect and actively misleading for an educational explorer — a user could reasonably conclude the funds there could still be spent.

Repro: visit /explorer/tx/caca5a48c62c196940b1236624e816475f8bafa582619ae25ff53d7a5c3d9731 (coinbase tx in block 956,591) and look at the second output (OP_RETURN, 0.00 BTC) — it shows "Unspent" like the first output.

Suggested fix: special-case OP_RETURN outputs to show "Unspendable" (or omit the spent/unspent badge entirely and show the OP_RETURN data instead).
close_reason: Fixed in 1f94da6: OP_RETURN outputs now show an Unspendable badge with an explanation instead of Spent/Unspent

title: Referrals: single-sig wizard device-picker + no-hardware-wallet helper
description: In src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/+page.svelte, add a 'buy' link (getDeviceBuyUrl) near each device card in METHOD_CARDS, and add a referral link in the existing 'no hardware wallet available' helper text (~line 191). Gate entire feature behind page.data.flags?.referral_links. Links open in new tab (target=_blank rel=noopener).
close_reason: Built + committed: schema/flag/tabs in 024542f, full feature (referrals constants + server helpers, /admin/referral-settings, wizard buy links, managed-service card, signer buy/troubleshooting links, wallet-detail Need-help expandable, 19 tests) in 8c974f7. Full suite + svelte-check green.

title: Route remaining plaintext secrets through secretKey.ts encryption
description:
docs/DATA-AUDIT-2026-07-06.md section 4 + section 9: notification_channel_config's ntfy access
token and webhook HMAC secret are stored in plaintext JSON, and the instance-wide `settings`
KV table stores smtp_pass/core_rpc_pass/telegram_bot_token in plaintext -- all four are
bearer-credential material that secretKey.ts's AES-256-GCM envelope (encryptSecret/decryptSecret,
src/lib/server/secretKey.ts) already exists to protect. Personal SMTP password (email.ts) and
nostr_sender_privkey (channels/nostr.ts) are already correctly encrypted with this same
primitive -- this epic makes the remaining four consistent with that existing pattern.
Audit calls this "the single highest-value fix" in the report. Children: A1-A4 below.
close_reason: All 4 children done: ntfy token, webhook secret, and the three settings secrets encrypted at rest via secretKey.ts envelopes; credential keys split into typed instance_secrets table with startup migration.

title: Encrypt ntfy access token at rest
description:
notification_channel_config stores the ntfy accessToken in plaintext JSON. Write path:
src/routes/api/notifications/channels/[channel]/+server.ts, the 'ntfy' case (lines 209-221) --
currently `if (accessToken) cfg.accessToken = accessToken;` with no encryption, unlike the
personal-SMTP password handled by buildEmailSmtp() 40 lines below (same file, lines 263-292),
which calls encryptSecret(rawPass) from src/lib/server/secretKey.ts and stores the result as
passEnc. Consumer: src/lib/server/channels/ntfy.ts reads cfg.accessToken directly (lines 80-81)
and uses it in the Authorization header (line 95).

Fix (mirror the SMTP pattern exactly):
1. In the ntfy case (+server.ts:209-221), import encryptSecret from '$lib/server/secretKey' and
   replace `cfg.accessToken = accessToken` with `cfg.accessTokenEnc = encryptSecret(accessToken)`,
   keeping the existing blank-means-keep logic against `prev.accessTokenEnc` instead of
   `prev.accessToken`.
2. In channels/ntfy.ts, decrypt via decryptSecret(cfg.accessTokenEnc) before building the
   Authorization header (replacing the direct cfg.accessToken read at lines 80-81/95).
3. Update src/lib/server/notifyConfig.ts's redactChannelConfig() so its hasAccessToken boolean
   resolves off the new accessTokenEnc field.
4. Migration: existing rows have a plaintext `accessToken` key, not `accessTokenEnc`. Add a
   one-time guarded migration in db.ts (following the codebase's existing guarded-ALTER
   migration convention) that walks notification_channel_config rows for channel='ntfy' and
   re-encrypts any plaintext accessToken it finds into accessTokenEnc, removing the plaintext key.

Acceptance criteria:
- A freshly-saved or migrated ntfy config has no plaintext accessToken anywhere in
  notification_channel_config (verify via a direct sqlite query in a test).
- ntfy notifications still send successfully end-to-end with a real accessToken; extend
  src/lib/server/channels/ntfy.test.ts to cover the encrypted round-trip.
- The settings UI still correctly shows "access token configured" for both freshly-encrypted
  and migrated rows.
close_reason: Encrypted at rest as accessTokenEnc (secretKey.ts envelope): save path encrypts, ntfy.ts decrypts (fail-closed), redaction off Enc field, startup migration re-encrypts legacy rows. Commit 8f7f955.

title: Encrypt webhook HMAC secret at rest
description:
notification_channel_config stores the webhook HMAC secret in plaintext JSON. Write path:
src/routes/api/notifications/channels/[channel]/+server.ts, the 'webhook' case (lines 233-247) --
`cfg.secret = secret` with no encryption. Consumer: src/lib/server/channels/webhook.ts reads
parsed.secret directly (lines 125-127) and uses it to sign outbound payloads with
createHmac('sha256', config.secret) (lines 159-160).

Fix (same pattern as the ntfy access token, see sibling bead):
1. In the webhook case (+server.ts:233-247), encrypt via encryptSecret() from
   '$lib/server/secretKey' and store as cfg.secretEnc instead of cfg.secret, keeping the
   existing blank-means-keep + minimum-length validation logic (the >=16 char check at
   lines 240-242 must run on the raw value BEFORE encrypting).
2. In channels/webhook.ts, decrypt via decryptSecret(config.secretEnc) before computing the
   HMAC signature.
3. Update notifyConfig.ts's redactChannelConfig() hasSecret boolean to check secretEnc.
4. Migration: guarded migration re-encrypting any legacy plaintext `secret` key found in
   notification_channel_config rows for channel='webhook' into secretEnc, following the same
   convention as the ntfy fix.

Acceptance criteria:
- No plaintext secret remains in notification_channel_config for webhook configs after
  migration or a fresh save.
- Outbound webhook HMAC signatures still verify correctly end-to-end; extend the webhook
  channel's test file to cover the encrypted round-trip.
- Settings UI still shows "signing secret configured" correctly.
close_reason: Encrypted at rest as secretEnc (secretKey.ts envelope): length validation on raw value pre-encryption, webhook.ts decrypts fail-closed, hasSecret redaction, startup migration covers legacy rows. Commit a81b416.

title: Encrypt instance-wide smtp_pass / core_rpc_pass / telegram_bot_token in settings table
description:
The loose `settings` KV table (src/lib/server/db.ts, CREATE TABLE at line 71) stores
smtp_pass, core_rpc_pass, and telegram_bot_token in plaintext, readable directly from a
copy of cairn.db. nostr_sender_privkey in this same table is already correctly wrapped with
encryptSecret() -- this bead makes the other three consistent with that existing pattern.

Write sites:
- core_rpc_pass: src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.server.ts:113-115
  (`setSetting('core_rpc_pass', rpcPass)`)
- smtp_pass: src/routes/api/admin/notifications/+server.ts:91-92
- telegram_bot_token: src/routes/api/admin/notifications/+server.ts:94-95

Read sites:
- core_rpc_pass: src/lib/server/settings.ts:69
- smtp_pass: src/lib/server/channels/email.ts:95
- telegram_bot_token: src/lib/server/channels/telegram.ts:129,146

Reference pattern already correct: src/lib/server/channels/nostr.ts writes
nostr_sender_privkey via encryptSecret() (~line 132) and reads+decrypts it (~line 146) --
copy this pattern exactly for the other three keys.

Fix:
1. Wrap each setSetting(key, value) write above with encryptSecret(value) before storage
   (skip encryption when value === '' , i.e. the explicit-clear case already handled at
   +page.server.ts:115).
2. Wrap each corresponding getSetting(key) read above with decryptSecret() before use.
3. Add `core_rpc_pass` to src/lib/server/logger.ts's REDACT_KEYS array (lines 180-205) --
   confirmed this is the one settings-table secret currently MISSING from that list
   (smtp_pass, telegram_bot_token, and nostr_sender_privkey are already present).
4. Migration: guarded migration in db.ts re-encrypting any plaintext values currently stored
   under these three keys, following the existing guarded-ALTER convention.

Acceptance criteria:
- `sqlite3 cairn.db "select value from settings where key in ('smtp_pass','core_rpc_pass','telegram_bot_token')"`
  shows only encryptSecret() envelopes (JSON with v/iv/tag/data fields), never raw text, after
  migration or a fresh save.
- Instance-wide SMTP send still works end-to-end (Telegram bot / Core RPC currently appear
  unwired to a live consumer per code search -- verify and note in the PR if still true).
- logger.ts REDACT_KEYS includes core_rpc_pass.
- Extend src/lib/server/settings.test.ts to cover the encrypted round-trip for all three keys.
close_reason: setSecretSetting/readSecretSetting wrap all write/read sites; core_rpc_pass added to REDACT_KEYS; startup migration re-encrypts legacy plaintext; settings.test.ts covers round-trip for all three keys. Verified: core_rpc_pass still unwired to a live consumer; telegram_bot_token IS consumed by telegram.ts. Commit b3610ed.

title: Split settings KV table into typed instance_secrets table
description:
src/lib/server/db.ts's `settings` table (CREATE TABLE at line 71) mixes plain configuration
(electrum URL, connection_mode, ntfy/nostr defaults) with credential material (smtp_pass,
core_rpc_pass, telegram_bot_token, nostr_sender_privkey) in one untyped key/value table --
"what's sensitive here" currently requires a full code audit to discover rather than being
obvious from the schema. Depends on the encryption fix (sibling bead: "Encrypt instance-wide
smtp_pass / core_rpc_pass / telegram_bot_token in settings table") landing first so the values
being migrated are already encrypted envelopes.

Fix: add a new `instance_secrets(key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value_enc TEXT NOT NULL, updated_at
TEXT NOT NULL)` table. Migrate smtp_pass/core_rpc_pass/telegram_bot_token/nostr_sender_privkey
rows out of `settings` and into `instance_secrets` (values already encrypted per the sibling
bead). Update src/lib/server/settings.ts's getSetting/setSetting call sites for these four
keys to read/write the new table instead. Update src/lib/server/backup.ts's buildBackup()
(lines 49-68) -- which currently filters `settings` via a SENSITIVE_SETTING regex -- to simply
exclude instance_secrets entirely from the backup export, by construction rather than by regex.

Acceptance criteria:
- `settings` table contains only plain configuration values after migration.
- `instance_secrets` holds only the four encrypted credential values.
- Admin full-instance backup export (backup.ts) never includes instance_secrets rows even if
  the SENSITIVE_SETTING regex were ever loosened -- verified by a test that adds a bogus key
  to instance_secrets and confirms buildBackup() output never contains it.
close_reason: instance_secrets(key,value_enc,updated_at) table added; setSecretSetting/readSecretSetting retargeted with legacy settings fallback; nostr identity key routed through shared helpers; startup migration moves all four keys; buildBackup excludes the table by construction (test-pinned). Commit f5d2a6e.

title: portfolio.ts silently drops failed wallet scans from dashboard totals, zero logging
description: src/lib/server/portfolio.ts:158-179: scanWallet(...).then(ok, () => null) and the multisig equivalent convert any scan rejection (e.g. Electrum failure) to null with zero logging, silently excluding that wallet from the portfolio dashboard's confirmed/unconfirmed totals. A user could see an incorrect (understated) total balance during a partial chain-data outage with no error indicator anywhere. Contrast with wallets.ts/multisigScan.ts's list endpoints, which populate an explicit errors map for the same failure class. Fix: at minimum add log.warn on scan failure; consider surfacing a "some wallets could not be scanned" indicator in the dashboard response. Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Fixed in 7ebd68c

title: Vault components hardcode accent/status rgba opacity instead of shared tokens
description:
Design-system audit found the vault feature (newest, least-scrutinized surface) repeatedly hardcodes rgba() breakdowns of existing color tokens instead of reusing them, e.g.:
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/+page.svelte:293 .vault-card { border-color: rgba(232, 147, 90, 0.25) } -- that's --accent at 25% opacity, hand-typed instead of referencing --accent-muted (which is 15%) or a new shared token if 25% is genuinely a different intentional weight.
- src/routes/(app)/vaults/[id]/+page.svelte:906,1015 -- rgba(232, 147, 90, 0.35) and 0.3 -- two more one-off accent opacities in the same file.
- src/routes/(app)/vaults/new/+page.svelte:1567 -- another rgba(232, 147, 90, 0.25).
- src/routes/(app)/vaults/[id]/send/+page.svelte:1753,1762,1935,1998 -- hardcoded rgba(232, 201, 90, 0.3) / rgba(232, 90, 90, 0.35) alongside var(--warning-muted)/var(--error-muted) in the same rule, rather than a single token-based border color.

None of these look visually broken today, but every one is a spot where a future palette tweak in app.css won't propagate, and it is scattered enough (5+ files) that it is drift, not a one-off typo. Either add the additional opacity weights actually needed as named tokens in app.css (e.g. --accent-border or similar) or standardize on the existing --accent-muted/--warning-muted/--error-muted everywhere and stop hand-deriving new opacities per component.
close_reason: Fixed in 0072dc7: five shared border tokens (--accent-border, --accent-border-strong, --warning-border, --warning-border-strong, --error-border) replace all hand-typed rgba literals across the wallets tree (verified zero rgba(232,... stragglers there). Appearance preserved (<=0.05 opacity shift on muted-fill borders). Dashboard/settings stragglers noted as out-of-scope.

title: Announcements: user-side banner rendering + dismissal
description: In src/routes/(app)/+layout.svelte (~line 90-132), render active/non-expired announcements sorted by display_order, styled by type, below nav and above {@render children()} -- same slot used by the existing backup/reminder banners. Non-dismissible banners always show while active. Dismissible banners show a dismiss (x) button; dismissal upserts into announcement_dismissals and the banner disappears for that user until re-shown (it isn't -- dismissal is permanent per announcement, matching backup_reminders semantics) or the announcement is edited/reactivated. Load list server-side in +layout.server.ts, filtered against locals.flags.announcement_banners.
close_reason: Built + committed: schema/flag/tabs in 024542f, full feature (server module, /admin/announcements CRUD with live preview + audit trail, layout banner rendering, dismiss API, 28 tests) in b674fbd. Full suite + svelte-check green.

title: Mobile: primary nav is a horizontal-scroll strip instead of a proper mobile nav pattern
description:
All pages, mobile viewport (tested 390x844)

What I did:
- Resized to a phone-width viewport and browsed the app.

What happened:
- The top navigation bar (Dashboard, Explorer, Wallets, Vaults, Settings, Admin) doesn't collapse into a hamburger menu or bottom tab bar on narrow screens — it just becomes a horizontally-scrolling strip of nav items, with a visible scrollbar underneath. It is fully functional (confirmed scrolling reveals every item, all clickable), just not a typical/expected mobile navigation pattern.

What should have happened:
- A more conventional mobile-first nav treatment (hamburger/drawer menu, or bottom tab bar) would be more discoverable and idiomatic. Right now a first-time mobile user might not realize the nav scrolls sideways since there's no obvious "there's more here" affordance beyond a thin scrollbar.

Priority: P3 — purely a polish/UX-pattern issue, not broken functionality. Noting for the design backlog.
close_reason: Addressed in 22ab66d by the same edge-fade scroll cue as cairn-peoj: the strip now visibly reads as horizontally scrollable. A full hamburger/bottom-tab redesign remains a larger design-backlog item, but the core 'no affordance' complaint is resolved.

title: Vault schema, creation wizard, detail page
description: vaults + vault_keys tables (key category Hardware/Mobile/Recovery, per-key xpub/fingerprint/path/name), server module + API, 5-step wizard (why+quorum presets 2of3/3of5/custom; add keys one at a time with per-device import sub-wizard; review + test address; confirm; done with fund/export-backup next steps), vault detail page (quorum display, key list, balance/addresses/history, receive QR), descriptor export/import UI.
close_reason: Shipped across three commits + API routes in e0f5a5e: vaultScan/vaultExport with exact-string ColdCard + Caravan-contract tests, 5-step wizard (all steering rounds: device-first key import, five-method picker, xprv refusal, Unchained/Casa copy, ColdCard registration step), detail page with backup prominence, nav/list presence. 525/525 tests, 0 check errors, full wizard walked live in the browser + 18/18 HTTP suite. Notes: Trezor/Ledger read seam degrades to paste until wired (drivers landed after its final run); Caravan golden fixture deferred to cairn-yxf; imported uuid/ledgerPolicyHmacs dropped (no storage).

title: Portfolio cache: persist scan results to SQLite for instant-but-stale reads across restart
description: Part (b) split out of cairn-fd56 (part (a), startup pre-warm, is done). Persist the per-wallet/per-multisig scan results (WalletScanResult / MultisigScanResult: balances, tx summary, scanned-address count) to a new SQLite table (e.g. wallet_scan_cache keyed by xpub / multisig descriptor, with updated_at) so a cold restart can serve a slightly-stale-but-instant result immediately, then refresh on the next 60s cache expiry / the warm pass. Requires: (1) new schema in db.ts; (2) a cheap single-upsert-per-scan write path in walletScan.ts doScan + multisigScan.ts doScan (NOT per-address — the load test flags synchronous DatabaseSync writes as the app's primary bottleneck, so keep it to one write per scan); (3) load persisted rows into the in-memory scanCache Maps at startup (in warmPortfolioCache/startPortfolioWarm, src/lib/server/portfolioWarm.ts) BEFORE the network warm pass, so early post-restart requests get instant data; (4) correct invalidation on wallet/multisig deletion + xpub change (invalidateWalletCache/invalidateMultisigCache must also delete the persisted row) so stale data for a removed wallet never leaks; (5) a UX decision on whether stale reads need a plain-language 'Updating…' indicator (consult Cairn UX philosophy — no raw timestamps/internals). Deferred from cairn-fd56 because it needs schema + a write-path change + a UX call, unlike the localized part (a).
close_reason: Built: wallet_scan_cache table + scanCachePersist.ts; one-upsert-per-scan write path in walletScan/multisigScan doScan; startup seeding in portfolioWarm.seedScanCachesFromDb before force-refreshing warm pass; invalidate deletes persisted rows; UX decision to omit stale badge documented. 6 tests pass, svelte-check clean.

title: SMTP credentials sent in cleartext when admin sets tls='none' with smtp_user/smtp_pass configured — no cross-field validation
description: src/lib/server/channels/email.ts's sendMail() builds the nodemailer transporter with requireTLS/ignoreTLS derived purely from smtp.tls, while auth:{user,pass} is set independently whenever smtp_user is populated — with no cross-check preventing tls='none' + credentials from coexisting. If an admin sets smtp_tls='none' (the file's own docstring even recommends this as a 'local dev tip' for Mailhog) while smtp_user/smtp_pass are also configured for a real relay, nodemailer issues the SMTP AUTH exchange — and the notification body itself, which can include tx-amount details when no PGP key is set — in cleartext over the network. POST /api/admin/notifications validates smtp_tls enum membership and other fields independently but performs zero cross-field validation rejecting tls='none' when a user/pass is present. Fix: reject (or warn strongly on) saving smtp_tls='none' when smtp_user/smtp_pass are non-empty, in the admin settings save action.
close_reason: Fixed in c110211

title: Admin recovery setup is documented as mandatory but not server-enforced
description:
src/routes/(app)/recovery-setup/+page.server.ts has a comment stating: "Admins CANNOT skip: the whole point is that the instance operator stays recoverable. If they somehow leave with recovery incomplete, ... re-entry lands them right back here." This is not actually true of the current code -- checked src/routes/(app)/+layout.server.ts and src/hooks.server.ts, neither has any recovery-completeness check or redirect. The ONLY enforcement is a "nagging banner" in Settings, which is a visual reminder, not a gate.

Concretely: an admin can complete first-passkey setup, get routed to /recovery-setup, and simply navigate to / or any other URL instead of finishing it -- nothing stops them, redirects them back, or blocks any action. This is a real, if narrow, gap since it is the ONE account this whole system exists to protect against lockout for (the instance operator, who has no one else to ask for access back).

Not a security vulnerability -- it does not let anyone bypass auth or reach data they should not -- but it is a real functional gap between what the code claims to guarantee and what it actually does, on a feature whose entire point is preventing an unrecoverable admin lockout. Fix: add the enforcement the comment already describes -- an admin with incomplete recovery setup should be redirected to /recovery-setup from other routes (a check in the (app) layout load function, or hooks.server.ts, gated on isAdmin && !hasRecoverySetup(user.id).phrase-and-codes-complete), not just nagged.
close_reason: done

comments.author: auditor
comments.text: Audit note: closed with reason 'done' (no detail). Backfilling: fixed in 72b91d1 — added a recovery-completeness gate to (app)/+layout.server.ts, redirecting an admin with incomplete recovery (no phrase or no codes left) to /recovery-setup from any other route, guarded against a redirect loop on the wizard itself and runs after the disclosure gate. Verified: correctly scoped to admin-only (matches the original design in 659e24c where only admins are blocked, other users get a banner), and /logout lives outside the (app) route group so an admin can't get trapped unable to sign out.

title: backup_missing / backup_stale notification events are fully wired in UI/prefs but never fire — no trigger point exists (Unit 8 unbuilt)
description: notifyTypes.ts's NOTIFICATION_EVENT_TYPES includes 'backup_missing' and 'backup_stale' (docs/NOTIFICATION-PLAN.md Unit 8), and every downstream layer treats them as real: notifications.ts DEFAULT_PREFERENCES has entries for both (defaulting to ['inapp']), activity.ts USER_FEED_TYPES whitelists both for the user's personal feed, NotificationPanel.svelte maps both to icons, and src/routes/(app)/settings/notifications/+page.svelte lists both as toggleable rows with user-facing descriptions ('A wallet was created with no backup ever downloaded.' / 'Your instance backup is older than the reminder interval.'). However, grepping the entire src/ tree for notify({type: 'backup_missing' or 'backup_stale', ...}) turns up zero call sites — nothing in the codebase ever fires either event. src/routes/api/admin/backup/+server.ts (~line 24) sets the instance setting 'last_instance_backup_at' on every successful backup download and its own comment says this is 'groundwork: the notification plan's backup_missing/backup_stale events read this same key' — but nothing reads that key back. There is no scheduled/cron job, no check-on-load, and no per-wallet 'backup ever downloaded' tracking implemented. Net effect: a user can enable email/Telegram/etc. for these two events in Settings and reasonably believe they're protected, but will never receive a notification no matter how long a backup goes undone. This isn't a regression — it's simply unbuilt (Unit 8 of the notification plan) — but it's a user-facing gap worth closing since the toggle UI actively invites users to rely on it. Fix: implement the missing detector (a periodic check — e.g. piggybacked on the existing key-health cron pattern in keyHealth.ts — that reads last_instance_backup_at and per-wallet backup-downloaded state, and calls notify() with backup_missing/backup_stale when due).
close_reason: Added src/lib/server/backupHealth.ts (daily scan, keyHealth pattern, registered in hooks.server.ts). backup_missing: fires per from-scratch multisig with no wallet_backups row past a 24h grace, owner-scoped, throttled 30d via new backup_missing_notified table. backup_stale: fires an admin broadcast (userId:null) when last_instance_backup_at is older than 30d, throttled via backup_stale_notified_at settings key; never fires if no backup ever taken. Both event types now have a real trigger point. Tests added.

title: Multisig rename broke all old /vaults/* URLs with no redirect (404, no data loss)
description:
Context: commit 78a2864 "refactor!: rename 'vault' to multisig wallet across the codebase" moved the feature from /vaults/... to /wallets/multisig/....

What I did:
- Navigated to /vaults (list), /vaults/4 (an existing, real vault detail page — "E2E cairn-a4k Vault", 2-of-3, 0.24999216 BTC), and /vaults/4/send, all while logged in as the vault's own owner (admin).
- For comparison, navigated to the new equivalent /wallets/multisig/4.

What happened:
- /vaults, /vaults/4, and /vaults/4/send all return a hard 404 ("This path doesn't lead anywhere") — confirmed these routes are genuinely gone from the SvelteKit route tree (checked the filesystem: src/routes/(app)/vaults/[id]/ now only contains a `send/_components` folder with no +page.svelte at any level; src/routes/(app)/vaults/new has no +page.svelte either).
- /wallets/multisig/4 correctly loads the exact same vault with all data intact (balance, keys, quorum, registration status) — so there is NO data loss, this is purely a dead-URL problem.
- Also confirmed the top-level nav no longer has a "Vaults" entry at all (replaced by "Activity"); multisig wallets are now presumably reached only via the unified "Wallets" list page.

What should have happened:
- A breaking URL change like this should ship with redirects from the old /vaults/* paths to their /wallets/multisig/* equivalents (SvelteKit makes this easy with a `+page.server.ts` `redirect()` at the old paths, or a reroute hook). Right now every previously-shared link, saved bookmark, or QR code pointing at an old vault URL is permanently dead with no path back — for a Bitcoin custody app, "the link to my multisig wallet stopped working" is a scary, trust-damaging experience even though the funds and wallet are completely safe under the hood.

Priority: P1 — not a security or fund-safety issue (verified the underlying data is 100% intact), but a real breaking change shipped with no migration path, on the exact feature this instance is being polished for (collaborative custody). Cheap to fix with redirects.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 2028b7c: 301 redirect /vaults -> /wallets and /vaults/* -> /wallets/multisig/* in hooks.server.ts, query string preserved. Browser-verified. (cairn-kfn was the duplicate bead for the same issue.)

title: Portfolio dashboard: pre-warm scan cache on startup + persist to SQLite
description:
Load test (docs/LOAD-TEST-RESULTS-2026-07-05.md, Scenario 7) measured ~4.29s cold portfolio load (245 synthetic wallets, ~17.5ms/wallet, serialized over the single shared Electrum connection), then 60s of fast cached responses. Confirmed by code read.

Aggregator: getPortfolioDetail(userId) in src/lib/server/portfolio.ts:144-264, called from GET /api/portfolio (src/routes/api/portfolio/+server.ts:13-19). It has NO cache of its own - every request re-scans every wallet via Promise.all over scanWallet(xpub) (bitcoin/walletScan.ts, line 161) and scanMultisig(m) (multisigScan.ts, line 179).

The actual cache lives one level down, per-wallet/per-multisig, in two separate module-level in-memory Maps:
- walletScan.ts:15 `CACHE_TTL_MS = 60_000`; scanCache Map at line 176; scanWallet() (182-195) cache-aside logic, keyed by xpub, storing a Promise (so concurrent callers within TTL share one in-flight scan; failures evicted immediately, not cached).
- multisigScan.ts:35 same CACHE_TTL_MS=60_000; scanCache Map at line 292; scanMultisig() (302-315), keyed by descriptor via cacheKey() (297-299).

Both are PURE IN-MEMORY (plain `Map`), lost entirely on server restart - confirmed no `portfolio_cache`/`wallet_scan_cache` table exists in db.ts. There IS a `balance_snapshots` table (db.ts:356-368, written by recordSnapshot() in portfolio.ts:61-84, throttled to once/hour via SNAPSHOT_INTERVAL_MS=60*60*1000 at portfolio.ts:26) but it only stores hourly confirmed-balance totals for sparklines - not full scan results (no addresses/tx history/unconfirmed deltas), so it cannot substitute as a live cache.

No pre-warming exists anywhere: scanWallet/scanMultisig are only called from request-serving paths, never from a scheduled job. Server startup init lives in src/hooks.server.ts (lines 16-44), which already starts bootstrapAdminFromEnv(), startNotificationQueueWorker(), startAddressWatcher(), startKeyHealthWatcher() - each wrapped in try/catch, idempotent, unref'd (per addressWatcher.ts's own precedent at lines 653-681: a 10s-delayed setTimeout for the first run, then a setInterval for periodic repeats).

Fix - two parts:

(a) Pre-scan on startup: add a new `warmPortfolioCache()` function (e.g. in portfolio.ts or a new file alongside addressWatcher.ts) that enumerates all users' wallets/multisigs and calls scanWallet()/scanMultisig() for each, populating the in-memory scanCache Maps before the first real user request. Wire it into hooks.server.ts following the exact existing pattern (try/catch, unref'd setTimeout delay so it doesn't compete with the Electrum client's initial connect handshake).

(b) Persist portfolio cache to SQLite: add a table (e.g. `wallet_scan_cache`) storing the last-known scan result (balances, tx summary, scanned-address count) per xpub/multisig-descriptor with a timestamp, so a cold server restart can serve a slightly-stale-but-instant result immediately, then refresh in the background/on next 60s cache expiry rather than blocking the first request. This turns 'cold after restart' into 'warm-ish after restart, corrected within 60s' - should specify whether stale reads need a visible 'as of Xs ago' UI indicator (consult UX philosophy memory - Cairn avoids exposing raw internals, so any staleness indicator should be plain-language, e.g. 'Updating...' badge, not a timestamp dump).

Complexity: medium. Part (a) is small/localized (mirrors an existing pattern). Part (b) is larger - new schema, read/write paths in walletScan.ts and multisigScan.ts, and a decision on incremental-update semantics (only re-scan from last-seen address index rather than full gap-limit walk, to actually speed up warm-but-stale refreshes).

Risks/gotchas:
- Startup warming for a user with many wallets will itself contend with the single shared Electrum connection (see companion bead on Electrum connection pooling) - warming N wallets serially/concurrently at boot could delay the Electrum client's readiness for early real requests. Consider throttling or deferring warm-up until after the address watcher's own initial pass.
- SQLite persistence adds a write path to every scan - keep it cheap (single upsert per wallet, not per-address) to avoid adding write contention noted elsewhere in the load test as the app's primary bottleneck (DatabaseSync synchronous writes).
- Multi-user deployments: warming ALL users' wallets on every boot could be wasteful if only one user is actually active; consider only warming the most-recently-active users, or making this configurable.
- Persisted cache must be invalidated/refreshed correctly on wallet deletion or xpub changes - don't leak stale data for a removed wallet.
close_reason:
Part (a) — startup pre-warm — DONE. New src/lib/server/portfolioWarm.ts: warmPortfolioCache() enumerates every distinct wallet xpub (scanWallet, user-independent) and every user's multisigs (listMultisigs → scanMultisig), warming the per-wallet/per-multisig 60s in-memory scan caches so the first portfolio load isn't a cold ~4.29s serialized scan (load test Scenario 7). Best-effort (per-scan try/catch), serial with a setImmediate yield between scans to spare the shared Electrum connection + event loop. startPortfolioWarm() is idempotent, unref'd, and deferred 20s (past the address watcher's 10s initial pass) so it doesn't compete with the Electrum client's connect/subscribe/baseline; wired into hooks.server.ts alongside the other startup workers. 3 unit tests (enumeration + resilience to a failing wallet scan and a throwing multisig-list). Typecheck clean.

Part (b) — SQLite persistence for instant-but-stale cross-restart reads — SPLIT to cairn-er1k: it needs a new schema, a per-scan write path (load test flags synchronous DatabaseSync writes as the PRIMARY bottleneck, so it must be one cheap upsert per scan), startup load-into-cache, invalidation-on-delete, and a UX staleness-indicator decision — a distinct unit from the localized part (a).

title: Collaborative-custody key-add UX: ask intent, derive BIP-45 for shared vaults / BIP-48 for personal
description:
Audit (2026-07-06) compared Cairn's multisig derivation-path handling against Bastion's (C:\dev\bastion) key-upload flow.

FINAL MODEL (per Alex, 2026-07-06 — see cairn-1kc3 for the validation-side
rules this UX work feeds into):
- Collaborative custody vault (shared with other people, built on-platform):
  every fresh-derived cosigner key MUST be BIP-45 (m/45'). Non-negotiable. When
  a hardware wallet is connected, Cairn auto-derives m/45' — the user never
  picks a path. cairn-fdlf.4.
- Self-made/personal multisig (one user, all their own keys): standard BIP-48
  per script type, unchanged, already correct in Cairn today.
- Manual xpub paste can't be verified against a live device — needs its own
  ask-intent-first, validate-prefix-after flow, with concrete "how to export
  from m/45'" instructions when the user declares collaborative intent.
  cairn-fdlf.5.
- Imported wallets (Caravan/ColdCard/etc.) are exempt from the "must be 45'"
  rule — they can be used for collaborative custody regardless of path; Cairn
  represents what's already on-chain rather than coercing it. Only cairn-1kc3.3's
  universal "no single-sig purposes" rule still applies to imports.

Verdict on Cairn's existing multisig derivation: single-sig vs multisig path
separation is CORRECT and cleanly implemented (src/lib/hw/common.ts:
multisigAccountPathIndexes vs singleSigAccountPathIndexes). The gap is UX/product,
not a security bug in what's already built: Cairn has no ask-intent flow, no
BIP-45 support at all yet (everywhere multisig is discussed today assumes BIP-48),
and no prefetch/registry mechanism. See docs/MULTISIG-DERIVATION-AUDIT-2026-07-06.md
for the original file-level audit (note: that doc's conclusion "don't introduce
BIP-45" has been explicitly superseded by Alex's 2026-07-06 decision above —
BIP-45 is now required, deliberately, for the on-platform collaborative case).

Bastion's pattern this ports from (public/js/views/wallets.js:770-773 checkbox,
wireDeviceConnect() :1211-1266, msDefaultPath() :1657): ask upfront whether a key
is for collaborative use, and branch which path(s) get derived based on the
answer. Bastion's own master_keys table (src/server/db/schema.ts:396-410) keeps
purposes ('44'/'49'/'84'/'86' vs '48' vs '45') as separate rows, never mixed —
Cairn's registry (cairn-fdlf.2) follows the same shape.

Priority: Alex has called this out as a priority — get all of the collaborative-
custody derivation-path work built. Beads under this epic implementing the
ask-intent + BIP-45 auto-derive flow (cairn-fdlf.4, cairn-fdlf.5) and the
single-sig-wizard prefetch (cairn-fdlf.1) are bumped to P1/P2 and scoped to be
directly actionable ("ready for Fable to build").

Scope for this epic:
1. Opt-in checkbox in single-sig wallet creation's device-connect step ("I plan
   to use this key in a collaborative wallet later") — on yes, do a second live
   device read at BIP-45 (m/45'), matching Bastion exactly. cairn-fdlf.1
2. New persistence: a "known device keys" registry keyed by (user_id,
   fingerprint, purpose), storing 45'/48'/single-sig rows as strictly separate,
   never-conflated entries. cairn-fdlf.2
3. Registry must include a share-opt-in flag (default off) on single-sig-purpose
   rows; every sharing/export surface defaults to exposing only multisig-purpose
   (45' or 48') path data, never single-sig. cairn-fdlf.3
4. Multisig wallet creation wizard: ask "collaborative (shared with others) or
   personal (just your own keys)" up front, then auto-derive BIP-45 for
   collaborative or BIP-48-with-script-type for personal when a hardware wallet
   is connected — no path picker exposed to the user either way. Should also
   check the fdlf.2 registry first ("we already have a shared key for this
   device") before forcing a fresh touch. cairn-fdlf.4
5. Manual xpub paste flow (multisig "Add keys" step): ask the same
   collaborative-vs-personal intent, show clear instructions for exporting the
   m/45' xpub from common hardware wallets when collaborative is declared,
   validate the pasted path's purpose against the declared intent, and
   warn/reject with guidance to re-export on mismatch. cairn-fdlf.5

Dependency/build order: cairn-alw8 (P0, single-sig fingerprint storage — shares
wizard code with cairn-fdlf.1, fix first) -> cairn-fdlf.2 (registry table) ->
cairn-fdlf.1 (single-sig prefetch) and cairn-fdlf.4 (multisig wizard ask-intent +
auto-derive) in parallel -> cairn-fdlf.5 (paste guidance, shares UI surface with
fdlf.4's multisig wizard) and cairn-fdlf.3 (sharing defaults, needs fdlf.2's
table to exist first).

title: Add opt-in checkbox to single-sig key-add: prefetch BIP-45 shared key in same session
description:
In src/routes/(app)/wallets/new (single-sig wizard device-connect step), add an opt-in checkbox similar to Bastion's ssShareOptIn (public/js/views/wallets.js:770-773): 'I plan to use this key in a collaborative (multisig) wallet later.' When checked, after the primary single-sig read succeeds, do a second live read at BIP-45 (m/45') — the SAME path Bastion reads (public/js/ledger.js:100-109, public/js/trezor.js:240-248 both call the device with a literal path override "45'"), not an account-level path (no coin_type/account/script_type — BIP-45 has none of those fields). Fail soft — toast if the read fails or is unsupported (BitBox02/Jade don't support it per Bastion's own gating), do not block the primary single-sig wallet creation.

FINAL MODEL (per Alex, 2026-07-06 — corrects two earlier drafts of this bead):
this prefetched m/45' key is NOT a dormant "marker" that's separate from the
real collaborative-vault key — per Alex's decision, BIP-45 IS the real cosigner
key used to build an on-platform collaborative multisig wallet. So this
prefetch has direct payoff: if the user later creates (or is invited into) a
collaborative vault with the same device, cairn-fdlf.2's registry lets that
vault-creation flow (cairn-fdlf.4) skip a second live device touch entirely and
reuse this already-read m/45' xpub. (Earlier drafts of this bead said the
prefetch should be BIP-48 instead of BIP-45, then said BIP-45 should be
"marker-only" — both are superseded; BIP-45 is both the intent-signal AND the
usable key, matching Bastion's actual mechanism 1:1.)

Sharing-default constraint: whatever this prefetched BIP-45 key is later
attached to (a registry row, a wallet-config export) must follow cairn-fdlf.3's
rule — single-sig path data stays private by default, only multisig-purpose
(45'/48') paths are ever shared with cosigners.

Dependency ordering: this bead touches the exact same wizard code (device-connect
step, src/routes/(app)/wallets/new) that cairn-alw8's fix must also touch (threading
{fingerprint, path} from the device read into wallet creation). Build on top of
cairn-alw8's fix, not in parallel with it, to avoid two changes fighting over the
same form/route. Also depends on cairn-fdlf.2 (the registry table this prefetched
key gets stored in).

title: Add known-device-keys registry table to remember prefetched multisig xpubs across sessions
description:
Cairn currently has no persistent registry of previously-read hardware device keys — every readMultisigKeyFrom*/readSingleSigKeyFrom* call is a fresh live query with no memory (confirmed by 2026-07-06 audit). To support cairn-fdlf.1 (prefetching a BIP-45 shared key during single-sig add) and cairn-fdlf.4 (the multisig wizard reusing a previously-prefetched key instead of forcing a fresh touch), add a table keyed by (user_id, fingerprint, purpose) storing xpub + derivation path, modeled loosely on Bastion's master_keys table (src/server/db/schema.ts:396-410, UNIQUE(user_id, xfp, purpose)) but adapted to Cairn's conventions.

Purpose values this table must store, per Alex's final model (2026-07-06, see
cairn-1kc3 epic): single-sig purposes (44'/49'/84'/86'), '48' (self-made/personal
multisig, script-type-aware), and '45' (BIP-45, the required purpose for any
on-platform collaborative/shared vault). All three families are legitimate rows
in this registry — the CRITICAL constraint is that they must never be treated as
interchangeable: never store single-sig and multisig xpubs (whether '45' or '48')
as rows of one generic, loosely-typed 'purpose' field that application code could
accidentally read across (e.g. a single-sig lookup accidentally returning a '45'
row or vice versa). Keep the type distinction explicit — e.g. a discriminated
purpose field validated against a closed enum (ScriptType | MultisigScriptType |
'45'), not a bare string, and callers must always pass the specific purpose(s)
they mean to read.

This is a convenience cache only; actual wallet records (wallet_keys/multisig_keys
tables) remain the source of truth and are unaffected.

title: Wallet-config sharing must default to multisig-only path exposure — single-sig derivation path never shared with cosigners by default
description:
Requirement from Alex (2026-07-06): derivation path info should NOT be shared
across connections by default. When sharing wallet config with cosigners, only
a multisig-purpose path (45' for collaborative vaults, 48' for personal
multisig — see cairn-1kc3's final model) should be shared. Single-sig path info
is private and should be optionally shareable, not default. Bastion had this right.

Bastion's model (confirmed by direct source read):
- `master_keys` (src/server/db/schema.ts:396-410) has a `share_opt_in` column,
  default 0 — a single-sig key's path is NOT exposed to any collaborative surface
  unless the user explicitly opts in per-key.
- Cross-instance federation sharing (`federation_shared_keys`,
  src/server/db/schema.ts:450-469) has NO derivation-path column at all — it's
  structurally impossible to leak a path through that surface, by design ("Stores
  ONLY xpub + xfp + a display label + an opaque handle... paths are deliberately
  never transmitted or persisted across instances (privacy: a path can leak
  account structure)").
- In-app cosigner viewing (`redactConfigForViewer`, src/server/routes/wallets.ts)
  strips every OTHER cosigner's bip32Path for a non-owner viewer, keeping only
  xpub/fingerprint and the viewer's own key's path.

Cairn today: no leak currently exists because single-sig and multisig flows are
fully separate (confirmed in docs/MULTISIG-DERIVATION-AUDIT-2026-07-06.md — "Hardware
key reads for multisig never reuse single-sig material"). Cairn also already has an
equivalent of Bastion's per-cosigner path redaction: `redactMultisigKeysForViewer()`
(src/lib/server/multisigShares.ts:280-289) strips a multisig key's `path` for every
viewer except the one assigned to that key. That part is already correct.

The gap this bead tracks is forward-looking, and becomes real the moment cairn-fdlf.1/.2/.4
ship: once the known-device-keys registry (cairn-fdlf.2) can hold a single-sig-purpose
row for a device fingerprint alongside its 45'/48'-purpose rows, nothing yet stops a
future feature (contact/wallet-config sharing, an export route, an invite payload) from
reading "give me everything we know about this device/fingerprint" and incidentally
including the single-sig row. There is currently no per-row/per-key "shareable" flag in
Cairn's data model at all (unlike Bastion's share_opt_in), and no product rule written
down that single-sig path data defaults to private.

Fix:
1. When cairn-fdlf.2's registry table is built, add a `share_opt_in` (or equivalent)
   boolean column, default 0, scoped to the single-sig-purpose row specifically —
   mirroring Bastion's column of the same name and same default.
2. Any code path that assembles data to hand to a cosigner/contact (multisig export —
   multisigExport.ts, contact-facing views, future "share wallet config" features) must
   default to including ONLY multisig-purpose (45'/48') path data. Single-sig path data
   must never be included unless the specific row's share_opt_in is set AND the feature
   doing the sharing explicitly asks for it — no "include everything we have on this
   fingerprint" shortcut.
3. Document this as a standing product rule (not just a one-off check) since it's easy
   for a future feature to reintroduce the leak by joining across purposes without
   thinking about it — e.g. a code comment on the registry table itself, similar to
   Bastion's schema.ts:450-459 comment block.

This bead has no code to fix yet (cairn-fdlf.2's table doesn't exist), so it's scoped
as a design constraint to build into cairn-fdlf.2 from day one, plus a standalone
checklist item for whatever future sharing/export feature touches the registry.

title: Multisig wizard: ask collaborative-vs-personal intent, auto-derive BIP-45 or BIP-48 accordingly
description:
New feature, filed per Alex's 2026-07-06 collaborative-custody model (see
cairn-1kc3 epic for the full rules this implements).

Today, src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.svelte's "Add keys" step
connects a hardware wallet and reads a key at a fixed BIP-48 path
(readMultisigKeyFrom{Trezor,Ledger,Bitbox02,Jade}(scriptType, account) —
src/lib/hw/{trezor,ledger,bitbox02,jade}.ts) for every cosigner, regardless of
whether the vault is being built alone or with other people. There is no
"collaborative vs personal" question anywhere in the wizard.

Required change: before (or as part of) the device-connect step, ask the user:
"Is this vault shared with other people (collaborative custody), or just your
own keys (personal multisig)?" — mirroring Bastion's ms.collaborative checkbox
(public/js/views/wallets.js:1905-1907) and its effect on msDefaultPath()
(wallets.js:1657-1663, `collaborative ? "m/45'" : BIP-48 with script type`).
Then, when a hardware wallet is connected for a key slot:
  - Collaborative declared -> auto-derive m/45' (BIP-45) for every fresh device
    read. No script-type branching, no path picker shown to the user — this is
    non-negotiable per Alex, and independent of the vault's eventual P2WSH vs
    P2SH-P2WSH script type (BIP-45 has no script-type subfield).
  - Personal declared -> keep today's behavior: BIP-48 with the vault's chosen
    script_type suffix (1' or 2'), via the existing readMultisigKeyFrom* calls.
  - Before doing a fresh device read either way, check cairn-fdlf.2's
    known-device-keys registry for an existing row at the matching purpose
    (45' for collaborative, 48' for personal) for this fingerprint — if found,
    offer to reuse it (e.g. surfaced from cairn-fdlf.1's single-sig-wizard
    prefetch) instead of forcing another live touch.

Once the intent is chosen for a vault, it should be locked for that vault's
remaining key slots (all cosigners in one vault share the same purpose — you
can't mix a 45'-purpose cosigner with a 48'-purpose cosigner in one collaborative
vault, since the wallet is either "shared with others" or "personal", not both).

Scope boundary (mirrors cairn-1kc3's epic-level clarification): this ask-intent
+ auto-derive requirement applies ONLY to vaults Cairn is deriving fresh keys
for in this wizard. It does NOT apply to importing an existing Caravan/ColdCard
config — imports keep whatever path they already have (cairn-1kc3 point 4).

Depends on cairn-fdlf.2 (registry table, for the reuse-check) and pairs with
cairn-fdlf.5 (the equivalent ask-intent + validation flow for the manual
xpub-paste path, since not every key slot is a live device read).

title: Manual xpub paste flow: ask collaborative-vs-personal intent, show BIP-45 export instructions, validate pasted path against declared intent
description:
New feature, filed per Alex's 2026-07-06 collaborative-custody model (see
cairn-1kc3 epic and cairn-fdlf.4 for the hardware-connect equivalent of this flow).

Problem: when a user pastes an xpub for a multisig cosigner slot (rather than
connecting a live hardware wallet through Cairn), Cairn cannot re-derive or
verify the path — whatever the user pastes (xpub + optional path/fingerprint)
is taken at face value. Today there's no guidance at all about which path they
should have exported before pasting, so a user building a collaborative vault
via paste has no signal to export from m/45' specifically rather than whatever
their device defaulted to.

NOTE on hardware-wallet support (verified 2026-07-06, corrects an earlier
overstated concern in this bead's history): Trezor Connect and Ledger's btc-app
libraries DO export an xpub at an arbitrary caller-supplied path including
m/45', with no purpose-field restriction — confirmed directly in Bastion's
working code (readKey({ path: "45'" })) and unaffected by anything in this
bead. That capability is exactly what cairn-fdlf.4's live-connect flow already
uses directly, so it has zero gap. This bead (fdlf.5) only matters for the
narrower case where the user is NOT going through Cairn's own live-connect —
e.g. no browser HW support (cairn-4b2b), an air-gapped/QR-only signer, or a
cosigner using entirely different software. In that specific case, Trezor
Suite's and Ledger Live's own standalone consumer apps do NOT expose a
"type a custom path, get an xpub" field in their everyday UI — that low-level
capability only exists in the connect library (which only Cairn's own code, or
a tool like Electrum/Sparrow, drives directly). So the fallback instructions
below must point users to a bridging tool, not to the vendor's own app.

Required flow, in src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new's "Add keys via paste"
path (and any equivalent single-key paste UI):
1. Ask the same collaborative-vs-personal intent question as cairn-fdlf.4
   (shared per-vault, not asked again per key slot).
2. If collaborative: tell the user they need the m/45' (BIP-45) export, and
   point them at a tool that can actually produce it for a manual paste,
   rather than claiming their hardware wallet's own app can:
   - Electrum: already defaults new legacy multisig + hardware-wallet setups to
     m/45'/0 — the most direct path for a user who already has it installed.
   - Sparrow/Specter: support a custom derivation-path override when adding a
     keystore, so a user can request m/45' explicitly even though it isn't
     Sparrow's own default (Sparrow defaults new multisig to BIP-48).
   Do NOT tell users to look for a custom-path field in Trezor Suite or Ledger
   Live's main UI — verify current per-vendor support before writing copy, but
   assume the answer is "bridge through Electrum/Sparrow" unless proven
   otherwise for a specific device.
3. If personal: keep today's behavior (BIP-48 path expected, matching the
   vault's script type).
4. Validate the pasted path's declared purpose against the declared intent:
   - Collaborative intent + pasted path purpose !== 45' -> reject with a
     specific message ("this looks like a [single-sig / BIP-48] key — for a
     shared vault you need the m/45' export; see the instructions above") and
     point the user back to Electrum/Sparrow to re-export from the correct path.
   - Personal intent + pasted path purpose !== 48' (or empty/"m") -> warn per
     cairn-1kc3.1's existing script-type-suffix check.
   - Either intent + pasted path purpose is a single-sig purpose (44'/49'/84'/86')
     -> hard reject per cairn-1kc3.3, regardless of declared intent.
5. If the user has no path/fingerprint at all (bare xpub, no origin info) — keep
   today's permissive behavior (unknown origin is allowed), but the collaborative-
   intent instructions should still steer them toward pasting a full descriptor
   (`[fingerprint/path]xpub...`) rather than a bare xpub when possible, since a
   bare xpub can't be checked against intent at all.

This is the paste-side counterpart to cairn-fdlf.4's live-device auto-derive —
same intent question, same target purposes (45'/48'), but validated after the
fact instead of derived automatically, since there's no device round-trip to
control it in the first place. Reuses cairn-1kc3.3/.1's validateDerivationPath()
logic for the actual accept/reject decision; this bead is specifically about the
UX around it (ask first, instruct with the right bridging tool, then validate
with an actionable error).

title: Missing regression test: resetInstance() leaves instance-wide events behind (cairn-5s8y / cairn-rko3)
description: cairn-5s8y and cairn-rko3 (P1, duplicate beads for the identical bug, both closed in 46dd16f) fixed resetInstance() (factory reset) leaving instance-wide events (user_id IS NULL) behind, contradicting the Settings UI's 'nothing else survives' claim. Fix confirmed present, no test exists. Add a test to src/lib/server/admin.test.ts: seed both an instance-wide event (user_id NULL) and a user-scoped event, call resetInstance(), and assert both are gone afterward.
close_reason: Regression tests committed in 834945e (admin.test.ts, notifiedTxidsCleanup.test.ts, settings.test.ts, api/admin/{settings,users,notifications}/server.test.ts). All green in full suite.

title: No dedicated mempool page with fee distribution histogram
description:
The Dashboard and Explorer pages both show mempool summary stats (unconfirmed tx count, mempool fees total, mempool size in MB, and a 4-tier recommended-fee table: Fastest/Half hour/Hour/Economy), but there is no dedicated /mempool page with a visual fee-distribution histogram (e.g. sat/vB buckets vs. vsize, the classic mempool.space-style chart). Confirmed via route glob and a direct 404 on GET /mempool.

This may be intentional/deferred scope rather than a bug, but flagging since the original test plan called out "mempool page — fee distribution, recommended fees" as an expected surface. Recommended fees ARE present and look correct; only the distribution visualization is missing.
close_reason: Fixed in 0046d11: /explorer/mempool with 7-band fee distribution, projected next blocks, contextual fee recommendations, and 2h backlog sparkline, linked from the explorer mempool strip

title: SSE new-block handler over-invalidates (re-scans whole portfolio every block)
description: The new live-updates feature (src/routes/(app)/+page.svelte, explorer/+page.svelte, explorer/mempool/+page.svelte) calls invalidateAll() on every new-block SSE event, which re-runs every load function on the page — on the dashboard that means re-scanning every wallet in the user's portfolio (see the related sequential-scan bug) on every single new block, whether or not portfolio data could plausibly have changed. Prefer targeted invalidate(url) for just the chain-tip/mempool data, or split load functions so portfolio isn't coupled to block-tip refresh. Depends conceptually on the portfolio N+1 fix but is a separate root cause (over-broad invalidation, not just slow scanning).
close_reason: Fixed in 6f1ce4f: chain loads declare a cairn:chain dependency and SSE handlers call invalidate('cairn:chain') instead of invalidateAll; the portfolio moved out of the dashboard load entirely (client-fetched from /api/portfolio), so new blocks refresh tip/mempool data without touching wallet scans

title: Recovery phrase (12-word, account-only)
description: On passkey add: generate 12-word BIP39-wordlist phrase (SAME wordlist as Bitcoin, but ACCOUNT recovery — copy must scream the distinction: 'This phrase recovers your Cairn account if you lose all your passkeys. It does NOT access your bitcoin — your bitcoin keys are on your hardware wallet.'). Shown ONCE on a write-this-down screen. Stored as salted hash only (scrypt like passwords; never plaintext). Recovery flow: enter 12 words -> hash-verify -> prompt new passkey registration -> access restored. Admin gets it automatically during first passkey setup.
close_reason: Shipped (fbb7efd): 12-word BIP39-wordlist ACCOUNT recovery phrase, salted-scrypt hashed, reusable, normalized (NFKD/case/spacing). Account-vs-bitcoin distinction hammered in every screen. 29 recovery tests.

title: Collaborative-custody Units 1-2 shipped correctly-written but entirely unwired gates — Unit 3 must migrate routes AND thread redaction through, not just swap getMultisig calls
description: Verified commit d7b915b (collaborative custody Units 1-2) is exactly as scoped: getViewableMultisig/getSignableMultisig (src/lib/server/wallets/multisig.ts:135,155) and redactMultisigKeysForViewer (src/lib/server/multisigShares.ts:251) are all correctly implemented (parameterized queries, correctly-scoped EXISTS subqueries, proper 404-not-403 shape, correct path-redaction logic) but have ZERO callers anywhere in src/routes — confirmed via repo-wide grep. Every existing route under src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/** and src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/** (detail page, psbt, transactions, transactions/[txId], broadcast, file download, descriptor/caravan/coldcard export, history.csv, address-detail, receive, utxo-mass, keys/[keyId]/verified — ~17+ call sites) still calls the old owner-only getMultisig(userId,id), including all of src/lib/server/multisigTransactions.ts's exports (getMultisigTransaction, listMultisigTransactions, buildMultisigDraft, attachMultisigSignature, broadcastMultisigTransaction, deleteMultisigTransaction). listSharedMultisigs (multisigShares.ts:199, meant to surface shared wallets on a collaborator's own /wallets list) is also never called. Net effect today: fails CLOSED, not open — a viewer/cosigner gets a uniform 404 everywhere and cannot see or sign a wallet shared with them at all (the feature is currently non-functional for its intended purpose), and there is no live data leak. This is expected/in-scope per the commit's own message ('Units 1-2 only') and the plan's unit breakdown (Units 3/5 are separate, not-yet-started units) — not a regression. Filing this specifically to flag the landmine for whoever does Unit 3: migrating a route from getMultisig to getViewableMultisig/getSignableMultisig is NOT sufficient on its own — every response that includes the key list (detail page, descriptor/caravan/coldcard export, history.csv, any API response embedding keys) must ALSO be threaded through redactMultisigKeysForViewer for non-cosigner viewers, per docs/COLLABORATIVE-CUSTODY-PLAN.md §6's own explicit warning to 'audit every such response shape explicitly rather than assuming one central serialization point catches all of them.' toMultisigConfig (multisig.ts:183), the shared funnel used by export/detail code, currently maps keys straight through with no redaction hook — this is the specific chokepoint to add it to, or every consuming route must redact individually with a test proving it.
close_reason: Verified fully resolved by commit 297ba2d (cairn-xkpd): all 17+ multisig routes migrated to getViewableMultisig/getSignableMultisig; exports (caravan/coldcard/descriptor/address-detail) signable-gated so cosigners get full keys; viewer-facing key responses (detail page + API detail) threaded through redactMultisigKeysForViewer; viewer-only routes (history.csv/receive/utxo-mass) emit no keys. Non-participants get uniform 404. Redaction chokepoint concern addressed.

comments.author: auditor
comments.text: Update: commit 63dddd6 ('Unit 4: sign-session roster + notify at creation and each signature') landed on top of the still-broken access gate this bead describes — it does NOT fix it (confirmed: per the plan's own unit table, the route-gate rewiring is Unit 3's job, not Unit 4's, so this isn't a missed dependency, just Unit 3 still not started). New concrete symptom now live: freezeRosterAndNotify() correctly identifies a shared multisig's cosigners and fires a sign_session_waiting notification with a working-looking link to /wallets/multisig/{id}/send?tx={txId} — but every route that link leads to (the send page, transactions API, PSBT/broadcast endpoints) still gates through owner-only ownedMultisig()/getMultisig(), so the notified cosigner gets a 404 everywhere. Users can now be notified about a signature they have no way to actually provide. Also: getRoster() (meant to expose the reconciled waiting/signed split to a UI per the commit message) has zero callers anywhere outside its own test file — the UI-facing half of Unit 4 wasn't built either. Separately confirmed as a non-issue: the notification payload itself only carries {multisigId, txId, collected, required}, no amount/destination, and assignKeys() already prevents a viewer from ever holding an assigned key — so no information disclosure beyond the 3-tier model's intent, just the reachability gap this bead already tracks.

title: docs/API.md documents wrong path for password registration endpoint
description: docs/API.md:38 documents 'POST /api/auth/register' as the registration endpoint. The actual route (src/routes/api/auth/register/password/+server.ts) is 'POST /api/auth/register/password'. Hitting the documented path 404s (confirmed via curl: POST /api/auth/register -> 404, POST /api/auth/register/password -> 201). Also src/routes/api/auth/register/options/+server.ts exists (passkey registration options) with no corresponding doc entry. Fix: update API.md to the correct path(s), and consider documenting the passkey registration flow (options + verify) alongside the password flow.
close_reason: Fixed in 779a374

title: Bitcoin Core RPC password round-trips to the client in plaintext
description: src/lib/server/settings.ts getInstanceSettings() returns coreRpcPass in plaintext. This flows straight into: (1) src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.server.ts load(), which SvelteKit serializes into the SSR page payload and (2) src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.svelte, which pre-fills a type=password input with value={data.settings.coreRpcPass}. It's also returned as plaintext JSON from GET /api/admin/settings (src/routes/api/admin/settings/+server.ts). Anyone with view-source/devtools access to an authenticated admin session (or anything that logs the page payload/API response) can read the stored Core RPC credential. Fix: never send the stored secret back to the client — return a boolean 'has value' flag or a masked placeholder instead, and only overwrite the stored value when the admin submits a non-empty new one.
close_reason: Fixed in cb72bcc: introduced getPublicInstanceSettings() replacing the stored Core RPC password with a hasCoreRpcPass flag; admin settings load, page markup, and GET /api/admin/settings no longer serialize the secret; blank submissions keep the stored value, with an explicit clear path

title: All destination address types + P2SH/P2SH-P2WSH vault scripts
description: Two halves: (1) verify+test sends to all six destination types (P2PKH 1.., P2SH 3.., P2SH-P2WPKH 3.., P2WPKH bc1q.., P2WSH bc1q.., P2TR bc1p..) from single-sig constructPsbt — addressToScriptPubKey in xpub.ts already decodes bech32m; needs explicit per-type construction tests incl. taproot output + addressFromScript p2tr reverse mapping for summaries. (2) extend multisig.ts VaultConfig with scriptType 'p2wsh'|'p2sh-p2wsh'|'p2sh' (default p2wsh, the recommended type): sh(sortedmulti) and sh(wsh(sortedmulti)) derivation + descriptor build/parse, BIP-48 script suffix 1 for both P2SH forms (Bastion convention). Taproot multisig explicitly out of scope. Emulator bead cairn-a4k must include a bc1p send.
close_reason: Shipped in two commits: six-destination verification with per-type tests (found+fixed flat output-vsize fee undercount on sweeps to p2wsh/p2tr and uppercase-bech32 client rejection; witness-v1 32-byte rule was already present and is now pinned), and P2SH/P2SH-P2WSH vault script types with per-type descriptor round-trips. 36 new tests; 422/422 passing, check 0 errors.

title: Referrals: register referral_links feature flag
description: Add referral_links to FEATURE_FLAGS registry (src/lib/server/featureFlags/registry.ts). Existing categories are wallet|hardware|notifications|upcoming; add a new 'marketing' category (small type union change) since referral links and announcements don't fit existing categories. defaultEnabled: true per convention. Wire requireFeature('referral_links') in any new server routes/actions; check page.data.flags?.referral_links in UI touchpoints to hide all referral UI when off.
close_reason: Built + committed: schema/flag/tabs in 024542f, full feature (referrals constants + server helpers, /admin/referral-settings, wizard buy links, managed-service card, signer buy/troubleshooting links, wallet-detail Need-help expandable, 19 tests) in 8c974f7. Full suite + svelte-check green.

title: RBF replacement-history link to a replaced tx dead-ends at a generic 404
description:
Page: /explorer/tx/[txid] — Replacement history section on an RBF-replacing transaction.
