Instead there are now 4 independent hand-rolled redaction implementations (redact() in settings/notifications/+page.server.ts, redactForClient() in api/notifications/channels/[channel]/+server.ts, publicNotificationSettings() in api/admin/notifications/+server.ts, and a 4th copy duplicated inline in admin/notifications/+page.server.ts). Traced every current secret field (ntfy accessToken, webhook secret, smtp_pass, telegram_bot_token) through all 4 paths and confirmed each is currently redacted correctly — not an active leak today. But the fragmentation is worse than originally flagged: 4 places to keep in sync instead of 1, with nothing in the type system forcing a future secret field to be added to all of them. Recommend consolidating into one shared getPublicChannelConfig()-style helper now, mirroring settings.ts's getPublicInstanceSettings()/hasCoreRpcPass, before a 5th call site or a new channel's secret field gets added.

title: Missing/inadequate regression test: admin overview wallet count omits multisig wallets (cairn-xqfb)
description: cairn-xqfb (P2, closed in c110211) fixed the admin overview 'Wallets' count only summing the single-sig wallets table, omitting multisig wallets. A test exists in admin.test.ts (describe('instanceStats', ...) around line 172-179) but its fixture has zero rows in both the wallets and multisigs tables, so it cannot actually distinguish 'sums both tables' from 'counts only one' — it would pass against the pre-fix buggy code too. Strengthen the existing test: insert one row into wallets and one into multisigs for different users, then assert instanceStats().wallets === 2, so the test would genuinely fail against a regression back to counting only one table.
close_reason: Regression tests committed in 834945e (admin.test.ts, notifiedTxidsCleanup.test.ts, settings.test.ts, api/admin/{settings,users,notifications}/server.test.ts). All green in full suite.

title: Hardware wallet signing
description:
Device-specific signing integrations, each plugging into the guided signing stepper framework (cairn-yph.6) and the PSBT infrastructure epic (cairn-yph). Per Alex: bring over all the hardware wallets from Bastion (C:\dev\bastion). Important scope correction from the Bastion survey: Bastion actually has Ledger, Trezor, and Jade signing implemented, plus BitBox02 (not requested here, FYI only) -- but ColdCard signing is NOT implemented in Bastion, only a descriptor-registration file export. ColdCard's real SD-card sign workflow needs to be built from scratch for Cairn, using the air-gapped/file-based patterns as a starting point rather than a direct port.

Suggested build order within P2 (all same priority per Alex's instruction, but this is the natural dependency-free-to-hardest ordering): generic file-based signing first (no vendor SDK, exercises the whole stepper+PSBT round-trip end to end and immediately usable with Sparrow/Electrum/any PSBT wallet) -> Trezor Connect (web SDK, no native transport complexity) -> Ledger (WebHID) -> air-gapped QR (SeedSigner/Passport/Jade) -> ColdCard (needs the most from-scratch design work).

Security pattern to carry over into every device integration below, from Bastion's psbt.ts applyPartialSignatures(): never blindly trust a signature a device returns. Validate the returned signature's pubkey against the PSBT's own bip32Derivation fields before accepting it as valid for a given input. This is the difference between a hardware wallet integration and a hardware wallet integration with a spoofing hole in it.
close_reason: Epic complete: all five signing methods live in the Sign step — generic file, Trezor Connect (5f2d59a), Ledger WebHID (87c6ba6), air-gapped QR/BBQr (677c767), ColdCard SD card (e1d3d52), wired in 0f59954+a092c0f. Every device routes through the server-side substitution guard; every driver validates returned signatures against the PSBT's own bip32Derivation pubkeys per the epic's security note. npm run check 0 errors, 275 tests passing.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Adversarial browser verification (for S5's audit ask re: keys never touching Cairn / signatures validated against PSBT derivation / broadcast gate actually blocking):

1. No private key/seed ever touches Cairn: confirmed across all 5 signer components (generic file, Trezor Connect popup, Ledger WebHID, ColdCard SD file, QR/BBQr camera+paste) — every one of them deals exclusively in PSBTs. None has any input field, prompt, or code path that accepts a seed phrase or raw private key. Key custody stays entirely on the external device/Connect popup in every flow.

2. Signature validation, not blind trust: live-tested by uploading a PSBT with a tampered output amount through both the ColdCard file-upload path and the QR manual-paste path (real oncall wallet, real /file endpoint bytes, modified via @scure/btc-signer). Both were rejected server-side: "The signed transaction pays different outputs than the one you reviewed. It was not accepted." Also tested uploading a still-unsigned PSBT — rejected with "This PSBT was attached but still is not fully signed," confirming the client only advances to Confirm after the server (assertSameTransaction + summary.complete) actually validates it. This matches the epic's stated security pattern.

3. Confirmation gate before broadcast: PATCH-attach and broadcast are two separate server calls (transactions.ts), each independently re-running assertSameTransaction + finalizePsbt — so even a client that skipped the UI gate couldn't get an insufficiently-validated PSBT broadcast. Didn't attempt a real mainnet broadcast (no funded test wallet available + out of scope for browser testing without explicit sign-off), but the guard logic itself was exercised directly against real code in a prior session's oracle proof (constructPsbt -> sign -> assertSameTransaction -> finalizePsbt, full pass).

Also found and filed cairn-sta (P2, non-security): ColdCard/QR signer cards don't proactively warn when a wallet lacks stored key-origin data, unlike Ledger/Trezor which do — walks the user through physical device steps for a predictably-doomed signing attempt. Everything else checks out clean.

title: Generic file-based signing (universal fallback)
description: Download the constructed PSBT as a file, let the user sign it in any external PSBT-compatible tool (Sparrow, Electrum, a hardware wallet's own desktop companion app), then upload the signed result back into Cairn to finalize and broadcast. This is the universal path that works for literally any signing setup, needs no vendor SDK integration, and should be built first since it exercises the full construction -> stepper -> signed-upload -> broadcast round trip end to end without any device-specific complexity. This is effectively the same mechanism as PSBT download/upload (cairn-yph.2) with signing-flow framing/copy layered on top -- do not build a second implementation of the download/upload mechanics.
close_reason: Satisfied by the guided stepper's Sign step (commit 40cf0d8) — the 'Generic wallet / file' method fully implements download-unsigned / sign-externally (Sparrow, Electrum, ColdCard, any HW companion) / upload-or-paste-signed. Per the bead, this is the same mechanism as yph.2 with signing-flow framing, not a separate build. Verified live end to end: .psbt download (200 octet-stream), signed upload normalized from both binary-base64 and hex, mismatch guard, unsigned->incomplete. This is the universal fallback every other device path falls back to

title: Trezor Connect integration
description: Web-based signing via Trezor Connect -- user approves the transaction on-device over USB, no file export/import needed. Bastion reference: C:\dev\bastion\public\js\trezor.js (452 lines), using @trezor/connect-web (remote-loaded JS, opt-in). Bastion has a server-side /trezor/prepare -> /trezor/apply seam -- understand why before copying it verbatim; may or may not be needed depending on how Cairn structures its PSBT signing request/response. Must include on-device address/amount verification in the guided flow (the stepper's Confirm step should tell the user to check the destination and amount on the Trezor screen itself, not just trust the browser UI).
close_reason: Trezor Connect signing shipped: driver src/lib/hw/trezor.ts (5f2d59a) with client-side PSBT->Connect translation (no server prepare/apply seam needed — Cairn parses PSBTs client-side), device-account-vs-bip32Derivation pubkey verification before attributing positional signatures, DER+sighash merge-back; TrezorSigner.svelte with on-device verify instructions; wired into Sign step (a092c0f). 27 unit tests; check+tests green.

title: Ledger integration (WebHID)
description: Web-based signing via Ledger's Bitcoin app over WebHID. Bastion reference: C:\dev\bastion\public\js\ledger.js (334 lines), using @ledgerhq/hw-transport-webhid + ledger-bitcoin. Note Bastion persists a per-wallet, per-device 'policy HMAC' (schema.ts wallet.ledger_registrations table) for multisig registration -- not needed for Cairn's initial single-sig-only scope, skip that complexity for v2 and revisit if/when Cairn ever supports multisig wallets. Must include on-device verification step in the guided flow, same as Trezor.
close_reason: Ledger WebHID signing shipped: src/lib/hw/ledger.ts driver + LedgerSigner.svelte (87c6ba6), wired into Sign step gated on isWebHidAvailable with disabled-reason fallback (0f59954). Policy-HMAC multisig registration skipped per bead scope (single-sig only). 25 unit tests; browser-verified by tester.

comments.author: fable
comments.text: Ledger WebHID lib + LedgerSigner.svelte committed (87c6ba6); Sign-step integration pending

title: ColdCard SD card workflow
description: Export unsigned PSBT to a file the user copies to an SD card (or via the newer virtual-drive-over-USB firmware, out of scope for v1 of this feature -- SD card only), signs on the ColdCard itself, then copies the signed PSBT file back and uploads it into Cairn. IMPORTANT: unlike the other four device beads in this epic, Bastion does NOT have this built -- it only exports a descriptor-registration file (coldcardMultisigFile() in wallet-manager.ts), no actual signing round-trip. This needs to be designed from scratch, not ported. It's mechanically closest to the generic file-based signing bead (cairn-ogw.1) -- same download/upload primitive -- the ColdCard-specific work is entirely in the guided-flow instruction copy (SD card insert/remove steps, PSBT filename conventions ColdCard expects) rather than new signing logic.
close_reason: ColdCard SD-card workflow shipped: ColdCardSigner.svelte file round-trip with guided SD insert/sign/re-import instructions (e1d3d52), wired into Sign step (0f59954). Designed from scratch as bead notes required (no Bastion port); reuses the download/upload primitive + substitution guard.

comments.author: fable
comments.text: ColdCardSigner.svelte SD-card flow committed (e1d3d52); Sign-step integration pending

title: Air-gapped QR signing (SeedSigner, Passport, Jade)
description:
Show the unsigned PSBT as an animated/paginated QR code (device camera scans it, fully air-gapped, no USB/file transfer), then scan the signed-PSBT QR the device displays back using the browser's camera (BarcodeDetector API or a JS QR-reading fallback for browsers without it). Bastion reference for the QR encode/scan mechanics: C:\dev\bastion\public\js\hw-signer.js (BBQr encoding via bbqrEncode + BBQrReassembler for the scan-back side) -- SeedSigner and Passport both use this same QR path in Bastion.

IMPORTANT scope note on Jade specifically: Bastion's actual Jade integration (C:\dev\bastion\public\js\jade.js, 534 lines) uses Web Serial (wired USB-serial transport, jadets library), NOT QR -- even though Jade the device supports true air-gapped QR signing as a hardware feature. This is a real decision point, not an oversight to just copy: Web Serial is proven/working in Bastion and is a faster port, but Web Serial has narrower browser support (no Safari, no mobile) than camera-based QR scanning, and true air-gapped QR is arguably the more fitting choice alongside SeedSigner/Passport in this bead's own grouping. Decide and note which transport Jade gets in Cairn before starting -- don't silently default to whichever is easier without that being a deliberate call.
close_reason: Air-gapped QR signing shipped: BBQr encode/scan lib + QrSigner.svelte animated multi-frame display, BarcodeDetector camera scan-back with per-frame progress and paste fallback (677c767), state_unsafe_mutation fix (d9d75c9), wired into Sign step (0f59954). Jade decision: camera QR (BBQr) not Web Serial — broader browser support, matches SeedSigner/Passport grouping. Tester verified full encode->sign->scan-back round trip with real BIP84 test vector.

comments.author: fable
comments.text: BBQr encode/scan lib + QrSigner.svelte committed (677c767); Sign-step integration pending. Jade transport decision: camera QR (BBQr), not Web Serial — matches SeedSigner/Passport grouping and broader browser support

title: No SOCKS5/Tor proxy support for the Electrum/Esplora chain backend
description:
Found during a privacy-tools parity audit against Bastion (2026-07-06) — neither codebase has this, but it's a real, material gap for Cairn specifically given its stated privacy-conscious design philosophy (plain language, no exposed internals, user-controlled backups).

src/lib/server/electrum/client.ts connects to the configured Electrum server via raw tls.TLSSocket/net.Socket (no proxy support at all). src/lib/server/settings.ts's electrum config (electrumHost/electrumPort/electrumTls/electrumTlsInsecure) has no proxy fields. Same story for the Esplora HTTP path.

Impact: every address/balance/UTXO query Cairn makes leaks the operator's real IP address to whichever Electrum/Esplora server is configured — including third-party public servers if the operator points at one instead of running their own node. A self-hosted Bitcoin wallet with no Tor option is a meaningful privacy gap; most privacy-oriented wallets (Sparrow, Electrum itself, Wasabi) offer at least a SOCKS5 proxy setting for exactly this reason.

Recommended fix: add a socks5Host/socks5Port (or torProxy) field to instance settings, and wrap the Electrum TCP connection (and Esplora HTTP client) in a SOCKS5 CONNECT handshake when set — a well-trodden pattern (e.g. the 'socks' npm package, or a raw SOCKS5 client, ~100 lines) that doesn't require bundling Tor itself, just the ability to point at a local tor SOCKS proxy (default 127.0.0.1:9050) or any other SOCKS5 proxy.
close_reason: Added SOCKS5/Tor proxy support for the chain backend end-to-end. Settings: new socks5Host/socks5Port on InstanceSettings + settings.ts (socks5_host/socks5_port), surfaced in getChainConfig() in BOTH public and custom modes (proxy is independent of connection mode). Electrum: ElectrumClient dials via socks.SocksClient CONNECT when a proxy is set, with TLS negotiated end-to-end over the tunnel (proxy sees only ciphertext); hostname sent to proxy for remote DNS (no leak). Esplora: EsploraApi routes fetch through socks-proxy-agent (socks5h://, remote DNS) via a node http/https path since global fetch/undici can't take a SOCKS dispatcher. Admin UI: new 'Privacy: SOCKS5/Tor proxy' field group on /admin/settings (always visible), with validation in both the form action and the JSON API; Test buttons exercise the proxy too. Declared socks + socks-proxy-agent as explicit deps. Tests: 2 new Electrum E2E tests (dials through an in-test SOCKS5 proxy; errors when proxy unreachable). Full suite 1059 pass, typecheck clean. Note: in-browser admin-page verification not driven (admin-gated); relied on svelte-check + unit tests per this branch's build constraints.

title: Undefined danger/danger-muted design tokens with wrong-semantic fallbacks
description:
Confirmed by grep: var(--danger, ...) and var(--danger-muted, ...) are used in 4 places across 3 files, but --danger/--danger-muted are never defined in src/app.css. CSS custom-property fallback silently kicks in, with inconsistent results per call site:
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte:1454 and src/routes/(app)/vaults/[id]/send/+page.svelte:1423 -- color: var(--danger, var(--text)) -- a "danger" indicator silently renders as plain neutral text, no red/alarm color at all.
- src/routes/(app)/vaults/stateless/+page.svelte:1486,1491 -- var(--danger-muted, var(--warning-muted)) and var(--danger, var(--warning)) -- renders as yellow warning instead of red danger, semantically wrong severity.

This is not new to vaults -- the wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte usage predates the vault feature, it was just never caught until this audit went looking for it specifically. Fix: add real --danger / --danger-muted tokens to app.css (most likely just alias them to the existing --error / --error-muted tokens, since "danger" and "error" are the same semantic red status here -- decide whether to standardize naming on --error everywhere and drop the --danger references, or keep both names as aliases for readability at call sites).
close_reason: Fixed in d5f9c73: added --danger and --danger-muted to src/app.css as aliases of --error/--error-muted, so the var(--danger)/var(--danger-muted) call sites resolve to the correct red instead of falling back to neutral text or yellow warning.

title: Descriptor import hardcodes scriptType p2wsh — sh(wsh()) and sh() descriptors create a wallet with the wrong script type
description: src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/import/+server.ts parseSource() and src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.server.ts import action both hardcode scriptType: 'p2wsh' for the descriptor branch, with a comment claiming 'parseDescriptor only accepts wsh(sortedmulti(...))'. That comment is stale: parseDescriptor (src/lib/server/bitcoin/multisig.ts) accepts sh(wsh(sortedmulti())) -> p2sh-p2wsh and sh(sortedmulti()) -> p2sh, and returns the recognized scriptType on the config. Importing a p2sh or p2sh-p2wsh descriptor therefore creates a multisig stored as p2wsh — every derived address is wrong, funds appear missing. Fix: use config.scriptType from parseDescriptor. Found while implementing cairn-1kc3.1 (the new 48' suffix-vs-scriptType check would falsely reject correct sh(wsh()) descriptors because of this).

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: API half FIXED in this change: src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/import/+server.ts parseSource now uses config.scriptType from parseDescriptor (and runs validateMultisigKeyPaths on the parsed config). REMAINING (why this stays open): src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.server.ts 'import' action (~line 225) has the identical hardcoded 'p2wsh' + stale comment — untouched here because the wizard's server action is shared surface with cairn-fdlf (Wave-A scope boundary). Note the blast radius is now smaller: even via the wizard prefill, createMultisig's new suffix-vs-scriptType check (cairn-1kc3.1) rejects the mismatched creation instead of silently storing a wrong-script-type wallet — the remaining bug is a confusing rejection, no longer silent corruption.

title: No educational tooltips anywhere in the explorer (block/tx/address jargon unexplained)
description:
Cairn is framed as an educational Bitcoin explorer, but block/tx/address detail pages surface raw protocol jargon with zero explanation: DIFFICULTY, NONCE, BITS, VERSION, WEIGHT, MERKLE ROOT on block pages; LOCKTIME on tx pages; address type codes like "P2WSH"/"P2WPKH" on address pages. None of these have hover tooltips, info icons, or inline help text.

Verified via code search: `grep -ri "tooltip|educational|help-text|info-icon"` across src/ returns zero matches — there is no tooltip infrastructure in the codebase at all.

Repro: visit /explorer/block/<any height> and hover over "DIFFICULTY", "NONCE", "BITS" labels — nothing happens. Same for address type badge on /explorer/address/<addr>.

Impact: for an app whose differentiator is explaining Bitcoin to newcomers, this is a significant gap versus the stated goal, not just a nice-to-have.
close_reason: Fixed in 0046d11: Term tooltip component with dotted-underline affordance now explains nonce, difficulty, bits, merkle root, weight, fees, vsize, locktime, RBF, SegWit, address types, and more across block/tx/address pages, plus collapsible How-does-this-work panels per page

title: Add-wallet wizard: hardware wallet option buttons (Trezor/Ledger/ColdCard/etc.) have no accessible name
description: On step 2 of Add a wallet (/wallets/new), the 7 key-source option cards (Trezor, Ledger, ColdCard, BitBox02, Jade, Air-gapped QR, Paste public key) render as <button> elements with no accessible name -- reading the page's accessibility tree shows them only as generic 'button' with no label. A screen-reader user would hear 'button, button, button...' with no way to tell them apart. Likely missing aria-label or the visible heading text isn't associated with the button role.
close_reason: Fixed in 22ab66d: the 7 key-source method cards now carry an explicit aria-label={title} so screen readers announce each by name (wallets/new/+page.svelte).

title: No LICENSE file in public repo
description: Cairn is a public open-source repo but has no LICENSE file. Task spec requires MIT license from commit one. Add a standard MIT LICENSE file at the repo root with the correct copyright holder/year.
close_reason: Fixed: MIT LICENSE added at repo root in cb72bcc

title: Merge Ledger signer pair (LedgerSigner/MultisigLedgerSigner + StatelessLedgerSigner) into one component
description: Follow the merged Trezor pattern (src/lib/components/signing/TrezorSigner.svelte, multisig?: MultisigContext prop). LedgerSigner.svelte (339 lines) vs MultisigLedgerSigner.svelte (666) — the multisig variant adds the on-device BIP-388 wallet-policy registration flow (ledger-registration API) and wrong-device probing; StatelessLedgerSigner.svelte (wallets/multisig/stateless/_components) duplicates much of it and should fold in too. Signing UI: preserve all copy/flows/error handling exactly; verify with svelte-check + vitest + loading both send pages in preview. Ideally hardware-test before shipping.
close_reason: Merged into src/lib/components/signing/LedgerSigner.svelte (multisig?: MultisigContext with optional multisigId for persistent vs stateless registration); StatelessLedgerSigner folded in; all 3 call sites updated; svelte-check 0 errors, 1120 vitest passing

title: Mobile nav (horizontal scroll tab bar) has no visual affordance hinting more items are off-screen
description: On viewports <=768px, src/routes/(app)/+layout.svelte:338-359 turns the sidebar into a horizontally-scrollable row of nav items (overflow-x: auto). At 375px width, 'Settings' (and part of 'Activity') sit fully off-screen with no fade/gradient/arrow cue that the bar scrolls. Functional (verified via scrollWidth vs clientWidth: 556 vs 375) but not very discoverable -- a first-time mobile user may not realize Settings exists. Low priority polish: consider a subtle edge-fade mask or scroll-shadow indicator.
close_reason: Fixed in 22ab66d: mobile nav strip now has a dynamic edge-fade scroll cue (bg covers pinned local + fades pinned scroll) so off-screen items are discoverable ((app)/+layout.svelte).

title: Referrals: tests for fallback precedence + flag gating
description: Test getDeviceBuyUrl() precedence (admin override present -> use it; blank -> fall back to official store constant). Test multisig_service_referrals CRUD + active-row filtering. Test that all referral UI (not troubleshooting links) disappears when referral_links flag is off.
close_reason: Built + committed: schema/flag/tabs in 024542f, full feature (referrals constants + server helpers, /admin/referral-settings, wizard buy links, managed-service card, signer buy/troubleshooting links, wallet-detail Need-help expandable, 19 tests) in 8c974f7. Full suite + svelte-check green.

title: Missing regression test: notify() dropping external-channel delivery on partial failure (cairn-s0p5)
description: cairn-s0p5 (P1, closed in 46dd16f) fixed notify() so a failure partway through (e.g. the in-app activity DB insert succeeding but external-channel enqueue throwing) doesn't silently drop tx_large/security_failed_login/admin_restore/backup_missing/key_health_due alerts. Current src/lib/server/notifications.test.ts does not cover this specific partial-failure scenario. Add a test to notifications.test.ts: mock recordActivity() (or the in-app write) to throw partway through notify(), and assert external channels are still enqueued/attempted independently rather than the whole call aborting silently.
close_reason: Committed in 8603ac6: multisigShares.test.ts (first coverage), contacts.test.ts share-revocation, notifications.test.ts stage isolation, broadcast route malformed-JSON 400 + log.error tests, walletsDraft.test.ts buildDraft/coin-control coverage.

title: Mandatory recovery setup UX after first passkey
description: After first passkey add: MANDATORY interstitial — 'Before we continue, save your recovery phrase' with 'I've written this down in a safe place' checkbox, then recovery codes with Download. Admins CANNOT skip; regular users may skip but get a persistent warning until set up.
close_reason: Shipped (659e24c): mandatory /recovery-setup wizard after first passkey (phrase behind write-it-down gate, then downloadable codes), admin no-skip, persistent settings banner until complete, secrets shown once. Caveat: forced first-passkey redirect emits next:'/recovery-setup' but no passkey-signup UI consumes it yet — enforcement rests on the banner + wizard; noted for when passkey signup lands.

title: Signing-mass device profile constants duplicated between client and server
description: src/lib/server/bitcoin/signingMass.ts defines DEVICE_PROFILES (base/perInput/rate constants per hardware device, used to estimate signing time for large multisig parent transactions); src/routes/(app)/vaults/[id]/send/+page.svelte separately hardcodes a client-side MIRROR_PROFILES (~lines 289-293) matching the same values, presumably so the UI can render an estimate without a round trip. These will silently drift if one is updated without the other -- there's no test or type-level guarantee keeping them in sync. Either export the server constants for client-safe reuse (if they don't depend on server-only code) or add a test that asserts the two constant sets stay identical.
close_reason: Fixed in 0072dc7: signing-mass device profiles + estimator math extracted to /shared/signingMass (environment-neutral), imported by both the server module and the client mirror; pure extraction, no numeric change (signingMass.test.ts still green).

title: Wallet detail backup-status indicator regressed for single-sig wallets (found auditing cairn-xvze)
description: cairn-xvze (closed in fd7427e) fixed wallet/multisig detail pages checking backup status via localStorage instead of the server-tracked wallet_backups table (isBackedUp()). While auditing cairn-xvze for missing-regression-test coverage on 2026-07-06, found that a LATER same-day commit (61e1b9d, 'feat(coinbase): coin-control maturity UI...') silently deleted the isBackedUp wiring from the SINGLE-SIG wallet detail page's load(), incorrectly describing it as dead state in that commit's diff. The multisig detail page is unaffected — only single-sig regressed. Currently the single-sig wallet page shows no server-tracked backup indicator at all, meaning a user could believe they've backed up a wallet (or vice versa) with no accurate signal. Restore the isBackedUp() wiring to the single-sig wallet detail page's load(), matching the multisig page, and add a regression test this time (e.g. asserting data.backedUp reflects the wallet_backups table) so a future refactor can't silently drop it again.
close_reason: Restored isBackedUp() wiring to single-sig detail load() + downloaded badge/optimistic overlay in the page; regression tests in page.server.test.ts pin data.backedUp to wallet_backups (incl. kind scoping). Commit d353f7a.

title: Missing regression test: Bitcoin Core RPC password no longer round-trips to client (cairn-g7a)
description: cairn-g7a (P2, closed in cb72bcc) fixed the Bitcoin Core RPC password round-tripping to the client in plaintext via admin settings, replacing it with getPublicInstanceSettings()'s hasCoreRpcPass flag. Fix confirmed present (settings.ts strips coreRpcPass; the admin settings page/route never serializes the secret; blank submissions preserve the stored value with an explicit clear path). No test covers this. Add a src/lib/server/settings.test.ts: seed a core RPC password via setSetting(), call getPublicInstanceSettings(), and assert the result has no coreRpcPass key and hasCoreRpcPass === true (and false when unset). Also worth a route-level assertion that GET /api/admin/settings's response body never contains the raw password string.
close_reason: Regression tests committed in 834945e (admin.test.ts, notifiedTxidsCleanup.test.ts, settings.test.ts, api/admin/{settings,users,notifications}/server.test.ts). All green in full suite.

title: Minor hardcoded values: button text color and border-radius bypass tokens
description:
Two small token-bypass instances found during the design-system audit, safe to batch:
1. src/lib/components/Stepper.svelte and src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/+page.svelte both hardcode color: #201409 (the dark-brown text-on-copper color) instead of a named token -- give this the same treatment as other on-accent text colors, or add an --on-accent token if it does not exist yet.
2. src/routes/(app)/vaults/stateless/+page.svelte (~line 1528, 1537) and src/routes/(app)/vaults/[id]/send/+page.svelte (~1817, 1824) use border-radius: 3px literally instead of var(--radius-chip) (4px) -- align to the existing token unless 3px is deliberately a distinct new size, in which case it should get its own token too rather than staying a magic number.
close_reason: Fixed: --on-accent token added and used in app.css/.btn-primary, Stepper, wallets/new stepper dots; stateless quorum-bar radii use var(--radius-chip). The bead's multisig-send 3px instances no longer exist post-rename; 3px uses in portfolio/explorer charts were outside the bead's scope and may be deliberate. Browser-verified: button renders identical rgb(32,20,9) via the token.

title: Redundant operator-name phrasing on /agreement when admin hasn't set a custom operator name
description: DEFAULT_OPERATOR in src/lib/server/disclosures.ts:17 is 'the operator of this Cairn instance'. The template at src/routes/agreement/+page.svelte:35 renders "This instance is operated by {operator}." which becomes "This instance is operated by the operator of this Cairn instance." for every fresh install until the admin sets a custom operator name in Admin > Settings. Awkward/redundant phrasing shown to every new user by default. Low severity, cosmetic copy issue. Fix options: reword DEFAULT_OPERATOR to something like 'its operator', or restructure the sentence to omit/rephrase gracefully when no custom name is set.
close_reason: Fixed in 779a374

title: Missing regression test: PUT /api/admin/settings has no validation (cairn-n31s / cairn-yzjq)
description: cairn-n31s and cairn-yzjq (P1, duplicate beads for the identical bug, both closed in 46dd16f) fixed PUT /api/admin/settings accepting unvalidated input that could silently misconfigure registration mode or node connection settings, unlike the equivalent form action. Fix confirmed present, no test exists. Add src/routes/api/admin/settings/server.test.ts (or extend an existing admin settings test): assert PUT with an invalid registrationMode/connectionMode/electrumPort/etc. returns 400 and does not persist, and a valid PUT returns 200 and persists — mirroring whatever validation the sibling form action already has tested.
close_reason: Regression tests committed in 834945e (admin.test.ts, notifiedTxidsCleanup.test.ts, settings.test.ts, api/admin/{settings,users,notifications}/server.test.ts). All green in full suite.

title: Test gap still open: SPV verification exercises only 4 of 6 InclusionResult reason codes
description: The 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit noted: 'SPV verification exercises only 4 of 6 InclusionResult reason codes.' Re-verified 2026-07-06: spv.test.ts exists (added as part of cairn-7zj6's fix) but still does not cover all 6 reason codes. Add test cases for the remaining 2 InclusionResult reason codes (check src/lib/server/bitcoin/spv.ts's InclusionResult type for which ones are currently unexercised) so every branch of the merkle-inclusion verdict is covered — this is the code path guarding against a hostile Electrum server forging payment notifications (cairn-7zj6).
close_reason: Committed in 26af83d: multisig substitution guard tests, legacy p2pkh/p2sh-p2wpkh spending-source tests, all 6 SPV reason codes, describe.runIf trap doors removed (multisig suites unconditional; regtest E2E skips loudly), coinbaseScan.test.ts fail-closed coverage.

title: docs/API.md has no documentation for multisig endpoints (/api/wallets/multisig/*)
description: docs/API.md documents no endpoints under /api/wallets/multisig/ despite there being 18+ routes there (src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/): the collection root, [id], caravan export, descriptor export, coldcard export, backup-pdf, psbt, receive, transactions (+ broadcast/file), keys/[keyId]/verified, ledger-registration, shares, utxo-mass, address-detail, history.csv, and the import endpoint. Single-key wallet endpoints get documented but the entire multisig surface is missing. Worth adding a section mirroring the single-key one, especially for /caravan and /descriptor since those are the two Sparrow/Caravan/Core interchange points a power user would reach for first.
close_reason: Fixed in 22ab66d: docs/API.md now has a full 'Multisig wallets' section covering the collection root, [id], import, receive, address-detail, utxo-mass, psbt, transactions (+file/broadcast/bump), caravan/descriptor/coldcard/backup-pdf/history.csv exports, ledger-registration, keys/[keyId]/verified and shares.

title: Balance-history chart shows stale snapshot after switching node connection, contradicting live 0 balance
description:
Observed live on the v0.1.3 Umbrel instance after switching Admin > Settings > Node connection from the default public servers to a custom regtest Electrum server: the Dashboard's "Portfolio" confirmed balance correctly showed 0.00 BTC / 0 sats (live, correctly re-scanned against the new regtest chain), but the "Balance over time" chart directly below it kept showing a flat line labeled 0.0000186 BTC on both the start and end of the visible range, contradicting the live number.

Root cause: balance history is stored in an append-only balance_snapshots table (src/lib/server/portfolio.ts), written by recordSnapshot() (throttled to ~hourly) and backfilled on wallet import by buildBackfillPoints() (added in commit 64c47af, cairn-ittq). Nothing invalidates or re-derives existing snapshot rows when the node/chain connection changes (or generally whenever a wallet's live balance changes to contradict its last snapshot) -- src/lib/server/dataRetention.ts only downsamples/prunes by age, never by correctness. getBalanceSeries() simply carries forward whatever was last snapshotted.

Impact: low severity, self-corrects within about an hour once the next snapshot is recorded, and this specific trigger (switching Electrum backend to a different chain) is a power-user action rather than routine flow -- but it does mean the chart can show a materially wrong number that visibly contradicts the live balance card immediately above it for up to an hour, which reads as a real correctness bug to anyone who notices, especially if they'd just switched to their own node for the first time (exactly the "point it at your own node" flow the settings page invites new users to try).

Fix shape: either (a) invalidate/re-derive balance_snapshots when the node connection settings change, or (b) have the chart refuse to show a snapshot whose value has drifted from a fresher live-scanned confirmed balance, falling back to its empty state instead of a stale number.
close_reason: Chose a variant of option (b), implemented entirely in src/lib/server/portfolio.ts (the node-connection settings path was out of this task's file scope): recordSnapshot() now bypasses the hourly throttle whenever any wallet's live, fully-scanned balance contradicts that wallet's latest snapshot (new contradictsLatestSnapshot() helper). Since getPortfolioDetail records the snapshot BEFORE building the series, the chart's newest point agrees with the live balance card on the very next portfolio fetch instead of up to an hour later — covering the chain-switch case observed on Umbrel and ordinary balance changes alike. Only complete scans reach recordSnapshot (existing guard), so a partial Electrum outage can never fake a contradiction. Growth stays bounded: extra rows only on real balance changes, and dataRetention's existing downsampling applies. portfolio/dataRetention tests pass.

title: Wallets empty-state 'Restore from a backup' link is identical to 'Add your first wallet'
description: In src/routes/(app)/wallets/+page.svelte (added in a816047, cairn-lun6), the empty-state now shows two buttons: 'Add your first wallet' and 'Restore from a backup', both linking to the same href="/wallets/new". Both land on step 0 (wallet-type picker) — clicking 'Restore from a backup' does not skip ahead to the restore-file-upload affordance (which only appears on step 1, the Key step, for single-sig). The two buttons are currently functionally indistinguishable despite different labels, which is confusing and doesn't actually shortcut the restore journey as the cairn-lun6 bead intended ('make the entry point discoverable from login/onboarding'). Fix: either deep-link to step 1 with the restore box pre-focused (e.g. /wallets/new?restore=1 read on mount), or open the file picker directly from the empty-state link before navigating.
close_reason: Fixed in 22ab66d: the wallets empty-state 'Restore from a backup' link now targets /wallets/new?restore=1, which lands directly on the Key step where the restore box is the first affordance (wallets/+page.svelte, wallets/new/+page.svelte).

title: resetInstance() factory reset leaves instance-wide events (user_id IS NULL) behind, contradicting the 'nothing else survives' claim in the Settings UI
description: src/lib/server/admin.ts resetInstance() runs DELETE FROM wallets/invites/sessions/users/settings in a transaction and relies on ON DELETE CASCADE (users(id)) to clean up everything else. But src/lib/server/db.ts L424 defines events.user_id as nullable with a comment 'NULL = instance-wide' — rows with user_id IS NULL have no FK target to cascade from, and resetInstance() never issues a DELETE FROM events. Confirmed by reading src/lib/server/activity.ts: recordActivity() writes instance-wide rows (userId omitted/null) for things like chain/electrum switches, backups, restores. src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.svelte's danger-zone copy tells the admin a reset 'deletes all users, sessions, wallets, and invites... nothing else survives' — that's false for instance-wide activity history, which a NEW operator (or the same operator post-reset) will see in /admin/activity, including entries like 'instance restore applied' from the prior operator/session. Fix: add DELETE FROM events; to resetInstance()'s transaction (or DELETE FROM events WHERE user_id IS NULL if any events should be considered structural rather than history).
close_reason: Fixed in 46dd16f

title: Missing regression test: admin self-disable/self-demote server-side guard (cairn-zl6d / cairn-vljv)
description: cairn-zl6d and cairn-vljv (P2, duplicate beads for the identical bug, both closed in 46dd16f) fixed the admin users route not blocking an admin from disabling or demoting their own account server-side (only client-side JS prevented it, unlike self-delete which already had a server check). Fix confirmed present at the admin users route (checks body.id === admin.id for both disabled:true and isAdmin:false, returns 400 before mutation). No test exists for this HTTP-layer guard — admin.test.ts only covers the lower-level setUserAdmin/setUserDisabled/deleteUser functions. Add a route-level test: POST to the admin users route as an admin targeting their own id with disabled:true (and separately isAdmin:false), and assert 400 + no mutation occurred, mirroring the existing self-delete guard's test if one exists.
close_reason: Regression tests committed in 834945e (admin.test.ts, notifiedTxidsCleanup.test.ts, settings.test.ts, api/admin/{settings,users,notifications}/server.test.ts). All green in full suite.

title: Missing regression test: portfolio.ts silently drops failed wallet scans, logging untested (cairn-ednl)
description: cairn-ednl (P2, closed in 7ebd68c) added logging when scanWallet/scanMultisig rejections in portfolio.ts get converted to null (silently dropping that wallet from the dashboard total). Fix confirmed still present post-warm-cache refactor at portfolio.ts:169-176 (wallet) and :187-190 (multisig). No portfolio.test.ts exists at all (portfolioWarm.test.ts covers a different function/scenario). Add portfolio.test.ts: mock one of several wallet/multisig scans to reject, call the portfolio aggregation function, and assert (1) log.warn fires with wallet/multisig id context, and (2) the failure is excluded from the total while other wallets' balances are still included correctly.
close_reason: Regression tests committed in 730319f (portfolio.test.ts new, backup.test.ts extended with logger spy, chain/chain.test.ts new facade coverage).

title: Extract shared gap-limit scan engine (gapLimitScanner.ts)
description: src/lib/server/bitcoin/gapLimitScanner.ts: runGapScan() with pluggable address derivation + ScanCache<T> replace the duplicated discovery loops, scripthash batching, tx attribution, 60s caches, and six-constant tuning blocks in walletScan.ts/multisigScan.ts. Public scan APIs unchanged.
close_reason: Committed in ce4ab68; svelte-check clean, full suite (1120) passes

title: All commits carry an AI co-author trailer — confirm this is intentional for the public repo
description: Every commit so far (14/14) ends with 'Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>'. This is a defensible, transparent choice (comparable to how Claude Code itself attributes commits) and I'm not flagging it as a defect — but since this pass was asked to specifically check for 'no AI mentions,' I want it surfaced as a decision point rather than silently left or silently scrubbed. Rewriting it now would mean rewriting all existing commit messages (destructive, history-altering), so I'm not doing that unilaterally. If the intent is to keep attribution, no action needed. If the intent is to drop it going forward, that's a one-line change to how commits are authored from here on (existing history would need an explicit rebase decision, separately).
close_reason: Confirmed intentional: Co-Authored-By trailers are deliberate, factual attribution on AI-assisted commits and are required by the contributing tooling's policy; they are commit metadata, not repo artifacts. Whether to strip them via history rewrite before first publication is the owner's call — flagged in the session summary

title: notify() can silently drop external-channel delivery after in-app record succeeds
description: src/lib/server/notifications.ts, notify() (around lines 172-206): the outer try/catch wraps both the in-app activity write and the entire external-channel enqueue loop. If the DB insert throws partway through enqueueing, the in-app record already succeeded but no external channel (email/telegram/ntfy/nostr/webhook) ever receives the alert -- visible only as one generic log.error line, and notify() returns void so callers have no way to detect partial failure. Affects tx_large, security_failed_login, admin_restore, backup_missing, key_health_due. rateLimit.ts's noteLoginFailure() calls notify() with no local guard, compounding the risk. Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Fixed in 46dd16f

title: Explorer
description: Block explorer covering blocks, transactions, addresses, the mempool, fee estimation, and universal search. The centerpiece of v1: every piece of data should be transparent and educational, so that someone new to Bitcoin understands what they are looking at after a few minutes of browsing.
close_reason: All explorer children complete: list, block, tx, address, mempool, search, educational framework

title: Block list page
description: Paginated list of recent blocks: height, age, transaction count, size, and fee range. Links into block detail. Height-based pagination (newer/older).
close_reason: Implemented in 1b1caab: paginated blocks list with height, age, tx count, size, fee range

title: Block detail page with educational annotations
description: Full block view: height, hash, timestamp, size, weight, transaction count, difficulty, nonce, bits, version, and merkle root — each technical field carries a plain-language explanation (e.g. nonce: the number miners searched for to solve this block's puzzle; merkle root: a fingerprint of every transaction in the block). Includes an introductory 'what is a block' panel, miner/pool identification from the coinbase, fee statistics (total, average rate, range) with an explanation of why fees exist, a highlighted coinbase transaction with subsidy + fees = reward breakdown, and a confirmations count explaining why more confirmations mean more settlement assurance. Paginated transaction list, coinbase first.
close_reason: Implemented in 1b1caab + 0046d11: full block view with plain-language tooltips on every header field, what-is-a-block panel, pool identification, subsidy+fees reward breakdown, coinbase explanation, confirmations context. Verified live on block 800000 (Carbon Negative, 6.25+0.1368768=6.3868768 BTC)

title: Transaction detail page with input/output flow
description: Transaction view built around the UTXO model: inputs on the left, outputs on the right, visually connected, with an explanation that Bitcoin tracks unspent outputs rather than account balances. Explains change outputs by analogy, shows fee as the input/output difference with sat/vB rate, indicates SegWit usage and the size vs virtual-size distinction, flags RBF (sequence-based) replaceability, and renders confirmation status as a 0-6 progress meter. Unconfirmed transactions get an ETA based on current fee rates.
close_reason: Implemented in 1b1caab + 0046d11: inputs-left/outputs-right UTXO flow with consumed/created framing, change-output detection with explanation, fee as in-minus-out, SegWit and RBF indicators, size vs vsize tooltip, 0-6 confirmation meter, fee-based ETA for unconfirmed

title: Address detail page with type detection
description: Address view that identifies and explains the address format (Legacy 1..., Nested SegWit 3..., Native SegWit bc1q..., Taproot bc1p...) with relative cost/efficiency context. Shows confirmed vs unconfirmed balance, total received/sent, transaction count, first/last seen dates, and a history table with per-transaction deltas and a running balance.
close_reason: Implemented in 1b1caab + 0046d11: address format detection with cost/efficiency explanations (Legacy/P2SH/SegWit/Taproot), confirmed vs unconfirmed with tooltips, total received/sent, first/last seen, history with running balance-after column

title: Mempool page
description: Dedicated mempool view introducing the mempool as Bitcoin's waiting room: unconfirmed transaction count, total size and fees, a fee-rate distribution visualization, recommended fee tiers with concrete time/cost framing, and projected upcoming blocks showing what will likely confirm next based on fee rate.
close_reason: Implemented in 0046d11: /explorer/mempool with waiting-room explainer, live stats (91k txs, 41.7MB verified), 7-band fee distribution, six projected blocks with fee ranges, contextual fee guidance, 2h backlog sparkline; degrades gracefully on plain esplora

title: Universal search
description: Search that classifies input (block height, block hash, transaction id, address), states what it detected rather than silently redirecting, offers guidance when input is ambiguous or invalid, and remembers the user's recent searches.
close_reason: Implemented in 1b1caab + 0046d11: classification states what it detected (looks-like-a-X card with view button) instead of silently redirecting, guidance on unclassifiable input, per-user recent searches with clear control

title: Educational UI framework
description: Cross-cutting components the educational explorer depends on: a term-explanation tooltip (dotted underline affordance, hover/tap to reveal), a collapsible 'How does this work?' panel with per-page copy that remembers open/closed state, and consistent number/timestamp formatting (BTC to 8 decimals, sats with separators, relative + absolute times).
close_reason: Implemented in 0046d11: Term tooltip component (dotted underline, hover/focus), HowItWorks collapsible panel with per-page persisted state, ConfirmMeter; consistent BTC/sats/relative+absolute time formatting via lib/format

title: Missing regression test: Trezor driver silently swallows multisig-change/cosigner-xpub parse failures (cairn-yaw1)
description: cairn-yaw1 (P2, closed in 7ebd68c) added console.warn logging (confirmed present at src/lib/hw/trezor.ts:1081,1202,1211) when multisig-change verification or cosigner-xpub parsing fails, instead of silently discarding the exception. No test asserts these warnings fire — trezor.test.ts only checks the resulting wrong_device error path. Add a test: spy on console.warn (or the logger, if migrated), feed a malformed cosigner xpub into selectMultisigKeyForDevice and a bad multisig-change scenario, and assert a warning is logged in each case.
close_reason: Committed in 81181db: Trezor warn-not-swallow tests, signPsbtWithTrezor + signPsbtWithJade (scripted device + PIN-relay fetch) coverage, sign-method list extracted to pure signMethods.ts with unit tests (capability-false renders disabled-with-reason, never vanishes).

title: Coin control: manual UTXO selection
description: Coin selection in constructPsbt (src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.ts, selectUTXO call) is always automatic/greedy -- there is no way for a user to pick specific UTXOs to spend from. Useful for privacy (avoiding linking unrelated deposits) and deliberate dust consolidation. Small-medium effort: ConstructParams would need an optional UTXO allowlist, and the Create step needs a UTXO picker UI (checkbox list, probably behind a disclosure toggle so it doesn't clutter the default flow).
close_reason: Shipped: onlyUtxos allowlist in constructPsbt (restriction of candidate set, not exactInputs; distinct no_utxos/insufficient_funds errors; send-max over subset tested), CoinControl.svelte disclosure with per-coin checkboxes and selected totals, UTXO list loaded from the cached scan. Live-preview verified: only the allowlisted coin was spent.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Verified with a real emulator round trip (commit 38679b2), per standing instruction to run PSBT-construction-touching changes through the regtest+emulator infra.

Funded wallet 4 with 3 distinct regtest UTXOs (30,000 / 68,530 / 20,000 sats). Two checks:

1. Restriction is real, not a preference: built a draft allowlisting only the 30,000 and 20,000-sat UTXOs. Selection correctly picked just the 30,000-sat one (sufficient alone) rather than needing both -- normal minimal-selection semantics preserved within the allowed subset, as designed.
2. Proved the allowlist is a hard restriction: built a draft allowlisting ONLY the 20,000-sat UTXO and requested 25,000 sats -- correctly rejected ("The selected coins don't cover that amount plus the network fee"), even though the untouched 68,530-sat UTXO sitting in the same wallet could easily have covered it. Coin control never silently reaches outside the allowed set.
3. Signed the resulting coin-controlled draft with the real Trezor emulator (genuine device round trip, same as the RBF verification) and broadcast for real -- confirmed via the real regtest chain.

No issues found. Full suite: 366/366 passing.

title: backup.ts restoreBackup() silently skips malformed/duplicate rows, zero logging
description: src/lib/server/backup.ts lines 241, 284, 305, 325 (per-row insert loops for wallets/multisig keys/Ledger registrations/addresses in restoreBackup) all catch and skip a malformed/duplicate row with only a code comment, no log call. An admin performing a break-glass restore has zero visibility into which rows were silently dropped or why -- the RestoreSummary just shows lower counts than expected. Fix: log.warn each skipped row with enough context (table, reason) to diagnose after the fact. Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Fixed in 7ebd68c

title: Missing regression test: multisig PSBT-substitution guard untested on the multisig path (cairn-973)
description: cairn-973 (P1, closed in 40cf0d8) added assertSameTransaction() to guard against a signed PSBT with different outputs than the reviewed draft being accepted/broadcast — wired into both single-sig and multisig upload/broadcast paths. src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.test.ts covers the single-sig path (recipient-swap, amount-change, signing-only-doesn't-trip-guard), but src/lib/server/bitcoin/multisigPsbt.ts's identical call site (around combineMultisigPsbts, confirmed still wired post the 881ee90 shared-handler-plumbing refactor) has NO dedicated substitution test — multisigPsbt.test.ts only covers an unrelated script-type-mismatch guard. Add a test: construct two multisig PSBTs from the same UTXO set with different recipients/amounts, and assert combining/finalizing draft A with a co-signer's PSBT for draft B throws the mismatch error. This is a fund-safety-critical gap on the higher-complexity (M-of-N) code path.
close_reason: Committed in 26af83d: multisig substitution guard tests, legacy p2pkh/p2sh-p2wpkh spending-source tests, all 6 SPV reason codes, describe.runIf trap doors removed (multisig suites unconditional; regtest E2E skips loudly), coinbaseScan.test.ts fail-closed coverage.

title: Invite creation silently clamps out-of-range count/maxUses/expiresDays with no feedback to the admin
description: src/lib/server/admin.ts createInvites() clamps count to [1,50] and maxUses to [1,1000] via Math.min(Math.max(...)) with no error/warning returned. The form at src/routes/(app)/admin/invites/+page.svelte has number inputs with min/max attributes but no enforcement of them (a user can still type an out-of-range value and submit). If an admin requests count=200 expecting 200 invite codes, they silently get 50 with no indication anything was capped - the returned form.created array (length 50) doesn't explain why it's short of the requested count. Fix: either reject out-of-range values with a form error (consistent with how registrationMode/connectionMode/electrumPort are validated elsewhere in this same admin area) or return a note in the action result explaining the count was clamped.
close_reason: Fixed in 22ab66d: admin invites create action now validates count/maxUses/expiresDays ranges and returns a form error instead of silently clamping (src/routes/(app)/admin/invites/+page.server.ts).

title: test issue please ignore
Referrals: schema + hardcoded resource constants
description: Add settings keys referral_device_<trezor|ledger|coldcard|bitbox02|jade>_url (generic settings k/v table). Add dedicated multisig_service_referrals table (id, name, url, description, logo_url, active, display_order, created_at, updated_at) since it's an admin-managed repeatable list. Add src/lib/referrals.ts with hardcoded OFFICIAL_STORE_URLS and OFFICIAL_SUPPORT_URLS maps per device (verify exact URLs before shipping: trezor.io, shop.ledger.com/support.ledger.com, store.coinkite.com/coldcard.com/docs, shiftcrypto.ch/support, blockstream.com/jade + help.blockstream.com). Export getDeviceBuyUrl(device) that reads settings override else falls back to constant.
